1	{"apiVersion":"tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1","kind":"TaskEnvelopeIn","taskUID":"a87f5f44-e404-4e11-97bc-4643bd078ce9","role":"planner","level":"plan","prompt":"## Outcome

Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

## Current foundation — ALREADY IN MAIN, build ON it, do NOT rebuild

Much of what early drafts of this run assumed it would build now ships in
main (milestone v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1). Treat all of this as
given infrastructure; the ONLY new product surface this run delivers is the
Codex subagent package + the per-level provider switch.

- **v1alpha2 schema + global Execution DAG (Phases 23-26).** `Plan`/`Phase`/
  `Milestone`/`Task` all carry `dependsOn` at any level; the assembler builds
  ONE global Execution DAG and derives Project-scoped waves
  (`tide-wave-<project>-<N>`) across plan/phase/milestone boundaries via
  layered Kahn. The project planner emits N Milestones up-front with
  `Milestone.dependsOn` (planning-DAG edge only — zero execution edges).
  Child CRDs authored by the planner are v1alpha2.
- **Global dispatch / failure / gates / resumption (Phase 25).** Readiness is
  re-derived from the global indegree map + completed-set each reconcile
  (nothing cached). `failureProfile` and the gate-as-hold model below are live.
- **Cost / budget / telemetry / cache (Phases 16, 18-21).** `pkg/dispatch.Usage`,
  `tide_cost_cents_total`, budget enforcement, and the dashboard Telemetry tab
  + cache-efficiency panel all exist. The locked metric label set is
  {project,phase,plan,wave}; the `task` label is FORBIDDEN (cardinality analyzer).
- **Dashboard (Phase 26).** A global execution-DAG view renders the whole-Project
  wave DAG (dagre LR); run #2's waves/spend are watchable live.

## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)

**Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
- internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
- internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
- internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
- internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern

All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.

**Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
- Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
- Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).

The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha2/project_types.go via
SubagentConfig). Wire the Codex image at the levels the operator wants Codex.
NOTE: today `resolveImage` honors a per-level image, but `ResolveProvider`
(internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go) still returns Vendor:"anthropic"
unconditionally — so a non-Anthropic image at a level is selected but then
rejected at runtime (the image's vendor sentinel expects an anthropic
envelope). Adding the per-level vendor/provider switch IS part of this run's
deliverable.

**Credential path.**
OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.

**Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
- Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
  independent).
- Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
- Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers. It is now
enforced by the global indegree model (Phase 25); do not add a parallel path.

**Usage accounting + cost — REQUIRED for budget + observability.**
The cost/budget/telemetry surfaces (Phases 16, 18-21) depend on every Subagent
populating pkg/dispatch.Usage. The Codex Run() MUST normalize the OpenAI/Codex
usage block into that struct, or budget enforcement, tide_cost_cents_total, AND
the cache-efficiency dashboard panel will all read $0 / blank for Codex:
- InputTokens        <- usage.prompt_tokens
- OutputTokens       <- usage.completion_tokens
- CacheReadTokens    <- usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens
  (OpenAI caching is AUTOMATIC, marker-free, process-independent, 1,024-token
  floor — there is NO cache_control to set and NO separate "cache creation"
  concept, so CacheCreationTokens stays 0. This differs from Anthropic, whose
  field is cache_read_input_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens.)
- EstimatedCostCents + CacheSavingsCents <- computed by a Codex-side pricing
  table mirroring internal/subagent/anthropic/pricing.go (per-model cents/MTok;
  cacheRead is OpenAI's discounted cached-input rate). Without this table the
  run cannot honor its own budget cap and the dashboard cannot attribute Codex
  spend. Keep the locked metric label set {project,phase,plan,wave} — the
  `task` label remains FORBIDDEN (metriccardinality analyzer).
The provider-neutral SharedContext field (Phase 20) on EnvelopeIn is shared
Go struct state — the Codex subagent receives it for free; no special handling.

**Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
- codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
  to stdout.
- Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
  --ignore-user-config.
- --json emits a JSONL event stream.
- --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
  — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
- Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
  (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
- API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
  no headless-OAuth problem.
- MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
  codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
- Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
  auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).

**Container image.**
internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent (replace "claude" with
"codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).

## Constraints

- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
  behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
  the controller or cmd/manager.
- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
  by Secret name only.
- Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
  controller-runtime patterns; keep make verify-* guards green.
- Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
  authoring any plan as delivered.
- Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
  guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.

content.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Working Rules

Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.

### 1. Observe First

Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.

1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**

Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.

### 2. Execute, Don't Ask

When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.

**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**

- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.

### 3. Verify Before Claiming

Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.

Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:

- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file
- **Gate APPROVED:** `grep -cE '^gate_decision: APPROVED$' <VERIFICATION>` returns exactly `1`
- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. (Phase 7 nearly tagged v1.0 on "14/14 green" while `make test-int` was red on a dropped chart-template block.)
- **A subagent's "pre-existing / unrelated" dismissal of a failing test is a claim, not verification.** Before relaying it as safe, git-archaeology the regressing commit (`git log -S '<token>' -- <file>`) and confirm it does not fail the gate. (Two Phase-7 agents waved off the dropped `podAnnotations` render block as pre-existing; it was a real ship-blocker — the verifier caught it and a `make test-int` re-run proved it.)

## Operating Notes (Phase 02.2 lessons)

Rules learned from 10 BLOCKED iterations across Phase 02.2. Trust them.

- **Don't predict "chain terminator."** Plans 02.2-08/09/10 each predicted the end; all surfaced new cascades. Frame work as iterations; don't anchor on terminations.
- **Pre-empt worktree cwd-drift before merging.** Executor agents occasionally write artifacts to main's working tree instead of the worktree's. Pre-merge check:
  ```
  UNTRACKED=$(git status --porcelain | grep '^??' | grep "<plan>-" || true)
  ```
  If non-empty, diff against the worktree branch's commits; remove duplicates before `git merge`. (Plan 02.2-09 merge failed without this; Plan 02.2-10 succeeded with it.)
- **`gsd-sdk query state.begin-phase` resets STATE.md body fields.** It re-derives `Current Position` and `Status` from current plan inventory. Edit body fields AFTER `state.begin-phase` runs, not before, or the edit gets clobbered.
- **Question implicit framings when cascade classes diverge.** If 3+ consecutive cascades are the same class (e.g. cascades 2/3 both flag-mismatches), trust tactical iteration. If consecutive cascades differ in class (e.g. cascade-4 budget → cascade-5 harness-bug → cascade-6 timeouts), surface "is the underlying assumption still right?" before authoring the next plan. The cascade-8 debug session forced this question 7 plans late.
- **Use `--wave N` filter when a closeout plan is gated on a BLOCKED artifact.** Plan 02.2-02's `checkpoint:human-verify` reads `gate_decision: APPROVED` from a verification artifact; `--auto` mode would false-approve against a BLOCKED gate. Filter dispatch with `--wave <new-gap-wave>` until any plan records APPROVED and the rewire fires.
- **Subagent prompts: focus, don't restate.** Agents have their own system prompts that cover anti-patterns and protocols. `<additional_context>` blocks should carry decision-only context (the specific gap, the user's choice, the corrected hypothesis) — not the full cascade history or the entire anti-pattern table. Token budget directly affects subagent quality.
- **Honor `workflow.auto_advance: true` literally** — fire the chain across phase boundaries. But ASK FIRST at fix-shape decision points (Option A vs B at scope-defining moments). The user memory note covers this; respect it.
- **Decorative banners are noise unless they signal a real workflow transition.** `━━━ GSD ► STAGE ━━━` belongs at PLANNING / VERIFYING / EXECUTING boundaries (per `references/ui-brand.md`), not on every status update. Routine progress can be one-line.
- **End-of-turn summary: one or two sentences.** What changed and what's next. Resist the urge to recap multi-section status when the conversation thread is fresh.
- **Use TaskCreate for multi-cycle workflows.** A plan-check-commit-execute loop repeated 5+ times in one session genuinely benefits from task tracking. The system reminders about TaskCreate are not boilerplate.
- **STATE.md "Current Position" body text drifts.** It's prose, not derived; updates to it need to happen explicitly. The frontmatter (`progress.completed_plans`) is computed by SDK verbs; the body text is not.

## The spec is load-bearing

Everything downstream — schemas, APIs, controller logic, persistence — should trace back to the paradigm doc. When designing or implementing, preserve these distinctions; they exist for reasons the doc argues for explicitly:

## Project

**TIDE — Topologically-Indexed Dependency Execution**

A Kubernetes-native orchestrator that runs hierarchical agentic coding work as a topologically-sorted DAG of subagent dispatches. A human applies a `Project` CRD (outcome prompt + target repo + creds); TIDE authors `MILESTONE.md`, phase briefs, `PLAN.md` files, and task diffs by dispatching specialist subagents at each level, parallelizing across waves derived from the declared task DAG. Built to be open-sourced and portable across clusters from day one.

**Core Value:** **The five-level paradigm (Milestone → Phase → Plan → Task → Wave) runs as a real K8s orchestrator that can drive its own next milestone end-to-end.** If everything else fails, TIDE-on-TIDE must work — that's what proves the paradigm and the implementation simultaneously, and it's the bar for "v1 ships."

### Constraints

- **Tech stack**: Go + sigs.k8s.io/controller-runtime + kubebuilder — K8s ecosystem default, idiomatic for CRDs/controllers, best contributor pool.
- **Tech stack**: Pluggable subagent runtime via a documented container image contract — never hard-coded to Anthropic SDK; v1 ships with a Claude-backed concrete impl behind the interface.
- **Distribution**: Apache 2.0, Helm chart from v1, designed for installation in arbitrary clusters with no hidden host dependencies.
- **Portability**: No hard-coded git host (GitHub, GitLab, Gitea must all work behind a generic git remote), no hard-coded LLM provider, no hard-coded auth model — abstract behind interfaces.
- **Persistence**: CRD `.status` only for v1 — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-object size stays well under etcd's 1.5 MiB hard limit by keeping per-Task CRDs small and label-indexed.
- **Failure semantics**: Wave boundary contract from spec §"Failure handling at wave boundaries" must be preserved exactly — failed task → siblings continue, dependents in later waves never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile. Resumption state = indegree map + completed-task set, nothing more.
- **Resumability**: Long-running agentic work outlives single context windows. Every level boundary is a saved artifact; a fresh orchestrator restart re-derives waves from the task DAG + completed-task set in O(V+E).
- **Observability**: OpenTelemetry tracing must use OpenInference conventions for LLM/agent spans so traces are queryable in standard AI observability tools (Phoenix, LangSmith, Arize) without bespoke instrumentation.
<!-- GSD:project-end -->

## Technology Stack

Pinned versions. See `research/STACK.md` for rationale, alternatives, and citations.

**Core:** Go 1.26 · controller-runtime v0.24.x · kubebuilder v4.14.0 · Kubernetes ≥ 1.33 (CEL CRD validation needs 1.29+) · Anthropic Go SDK v1.42.x · Claude Code CLI ≥ v2.1.139 · Helm 3 · Kustomize (kubebuilder-bundled).

**Supporting:** Ginkgo v2.28 + Gomega · envtest · kind v0.31 · logr v1.4 + zap v1.28 · prometheus/client_golang v1.23 · otel v1.43 (trace stable; metrics still v0.65 — keep metrics on client_golang) · OpenInference attribute names emitted on OTel spans (no Go SDK) · go-chi v5 · go-git v5.

**Dashboard:** React 18 + TypeScript · @xyflow/react v12 + dagre · Tailwind v4 · SSE (not WebSockets).

**Dev tools:** controller-gen · setup-envtest · golangci-lint (gosec, errcheck, staticcheck on) · helmify (Kustomize → Helm at release).

**Version coupling rules:**

- Never bump `k8s.io/*` independently — let controller-runtime's `go.mod` dictate.
- Pin Anthropic SDK to a minor (`v1.42.x`); it rev-bumps weekly with beta surfaces.
- OTel trace API is v1.x stable; metric/log APIs are v0.x — don't conflate the trains.
- Pin kind node images by `@sha256` in E2E scripts.

### Non-obvious choices to preserve

- **Layered Kahn in stdlib**, not Gonum or dominikbraun/graph. The spec's exposition is iteration-by-iteration; a graph library obscures it.
- **Native K8s Jobs**, not Argo or Tekton. The orchestrator owns the DAG; waves are derived, not declared as Workflow templates.
- **CRD `.status` only** — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-Task CRD stays small; the indegree map is in-process and rederivable.
- **OpenInference attribute names on OTel spans**, not the OTel GenAI semconv (still pre-stable in 2026). Phoenix/LangSmith/Arize consume OpenInference today.
- **CEL CRD validation (`x-kubernetes-validations`)**, not admission webhooks — except for cycle detection if CEL can't express all-paths.
- **zap behind logr**, not slog (~3× hot-path win for field-heavy reconcile logs).
- **chi router** as `manager.Runnable`, not gin/echo/fiber (fasthttp won't compose with controller-runtime's manager).
- **React Flow + DOM nodes**, not Cytoscape canvas. DOM lets every node be a real React component with live status.

### Anti-patterns

- Don't mount host `~/.claude/` into executor containers. Use `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env from a K8s Secret.
- Don't use Claude Code's OAuth flow headless (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100).
- Don't accept a wave list as CRD input — only a DAG. Don't cache the schedule in `.status` — rederive from the completed-task set.
- Don't hard-code one LLM provider, one git host, or one auth model in the controller. All Anthropic-specific code lives behind the `Subagent` interface in `internal/subagent/anthropic/`.
- Don't replace layered Kahn with CPM/HEFT. If pools become heterogeneous, add a wave-internal sub-scheduler behind Kahn.
- Don't add cycle "recovery" features. Refuse a cyclic DAG with a clear error.
- Don't vendor GSD Markdown. Re-implement planner/executor prompts as compiled-in Go templates.
- Default the chart's `ServiceMonitor` to `prometheus.enabled=false` to avoid CRD-not-found on plain clusters.

## Working Rules

Project-agnostic operating principles. Apply these before anything else, including before reading the rest of this file.

### 1. Observe First

Every wasted iteration in Phase 02.2 traced back to acting on assumptions instead of observations. The cascade-5 debug session hypothesized a "Wave Ready check" that didn't exist; the cascade-8 debug session hypothesized "ResourceVersion-conflict exponential-backoff suppression" when the actual cause was a never-assigned `Dispatcher` field. Both were avoidable.

1. When a runtime gate is BLOCKED → **read the manager log (`kubectl logs` or `/tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log`) and the VERIFICATION.md frontmatter BEFORE hypothesizing**
2. When a controller misbehaves → **`kubectl logs` + `kubectl describe <crd>` + `kubectl get <crd> -o yaml` BEFORE reading reconciler source**
3. When a debug hypothesis cites a specific code path → **grep the codebase to confirm it exists in the cited form** (`grep -nE "Wave.*Ready|Ready.*Wave" internal/controller/` would have refuted cascade-5's framing in seconds)
4. When a code change has no visible effect → **verify the binary rebuilt (image digest changed) and the Pod rotated (`kubectl get pods -n tide-system` shows new age)**, don't keep tweaking
5. When suggesting a Makefile target → **`grep -nE '^target-name:' Makefile`** to confirm it exists before recommending the command
6. When an agent could answer a question → **but `kubectl get`, `git log --grep`, or a single grep answers it faster, use the CLI**

Subagents are for understanding architecture and authoring artifacts. Runtime artifacts (manager logs, CRD status, git history) are for debugging runtime failures. Do not confuse the two.

### 2. Execute, Don't Ask

When the next step is obvious and non-destructive AND inside an active GSD workflow: do it. Don't present the command and ask for confirmation. This includes running `make test-int`, rerunning failing tests, creating planning artifacts, applying fixes specified in an approved `PLAN.md`, and committing per the executor's task protocol.

**Exceptions — always confirm or refuse:**

- **Production code edits outside an active GSD plan.** The `GSD Workflow Enforcement` section below is binding. Even if the fix is obvious, route through `/gsd:quick` or `/gsd:debug` so artifacts stay in sync.
- **Edits to `charts/tide/values.yaml`** — chart is FIXED contract per Phase 02.2's anti-pattern. Binary catches up to chart, never reverse.
- **`git push --force`** to any branch, especially `main`.
- **`git reset --hard`** outside a worktree's own HEAD-correction step.
- **`kind delete cluster`** outside an explicit clean-rerun sequence (it destroys all test state).
- **`rm -rf`** on `.git/`, `.planning/`, `.claude/`, or any directory containing uncommitted user work.
- **`gsd-sdk query state-set ...`** that downgrades phase status or rewrites tracked progress fields.

### 3. Verify Before Claiming

Never say "this should fix it," "the fix is complete," or "cascade-N is closed." Run the verification. Read the gate_decision. Grep for the artifact. Then state what you observed.

Concrete verification protocols for Phase 02.2-style runtime gates:

- **Plan landed:** `git log --oneline -1 -- <files_modified>` shows the expected commit on `main`
- **VERIFICATION.md exists:** `ls .planning/phases/<dir>/<plan>-VERIFICATION.md` returns a file

- **Test ran:** `tail -30 /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` shows Ginkgo summary line `Ran X Specs in Ys`
- **Dispatch is live (Phase 02.2-10 evidence shape):** `grep -E '"creating job"|"dispatch"' /tmp/<plan>-clean-run.log` returns ≥ 1 line, AND `kubectl get jobs --all-namespaces` shows Jobs in the test namespaces
- **Cascade closed:** the specific manager-log error that defined the cascade (e.g. `"no project found in namespace X"`) returns 0 in the new run's log
- **`make test-int` exit ≠ "Ginkgo green."** The `test/integration/kind` package bundles plain go-tests (e.g. the helm-template contract tests like `TestHelmDeploymentTemplateRendersManagerPodAnnotations`) alongside the Ginkgo specs. One RED go-test fails the package and trips `make test-int` non-zero **even when both layers print `Ran X of Y … SUCCESS!`**. Always read the echoed `MAKE_EXIT` AND `grep -nE '^--- FAIL|^FAIL\s'` the log — not just the Ginkgo summary. - **Layered Kahn in stdlib**, not Gonum or dominikbraun/graph. The spec's exposition is iteration-by-iteration; a graph library obscures it.
- **Native K8s Jobs**, not Argo or Tekton. The orchestrator owns the DAG; waves are derived, not declared as Workflow templates.
- **CRD `.status` only** — no external DB, no SQLite. Per-Task CRD stays small; the indegree map is in-process and rederivable.
- **OpenInference attribute names on OTel spans**, not the OTel GenAI semconv (still pre-stable in 2026). Phoenix/LangSmith/Arize consume OpenInference today.
- **CEL CRD validation (`x-kubernetes-validations`)**, not admission webhooks — except for cycle detection if CEL can't express all-paths.
- **zap behind logr**, not slog (~3× hot-path win for field-heavy reconcile logs).
- **chi router** as `manager.Runnable`, not gin/echo/fiber (fasthttp won't compose with controller-runtime's manager).
- **React Flow + DOM nodes**, not Cytoscape canvas. DOM lets every node be a real React component with live status.

### Anti-patterns

- Don't mount host `~/.claude/` into executor containers. Use `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env from a K8s Secret.
- Don't use Claude Code's OAuth flow headless (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100).
- Don't accept a wave list as CRD input — only a DAG. Don't cache the schedule in `.status` — rederive from the completed-task set.
- Don't hard-code one LLM provider, one git host, or one auth model in the controller. All Anthropic-specific code lives behind the `Subagent` interface in `internal/subagent/anthropic/`.
- Don't replace layered Kahn with CPM/HEFT. If pools become heterogeneous, add a wave-internal sub-scheduler behind Kahn.
- Don't add cycle "recovery" features. Refuse a cyclic DAG with a clear error.
- Don't vendor GSD Markdown. Re-implement planner/executor prompts as compiled-in Go templates.
- Default the chart's `ServiceMonitor` to `prometheus.enabled=false` to avoid CRD-not-found on plain clusters.

17	package anthropic
18	
19	// pricing.go holds the per-model Anthropic price table and the
20	// estimatedCostCents function that multiplies parsed token counts by it.
21	//
22	// This file stays inside internal/subagent/anthropic/ (provider-specific,
23	// behind the Subagent interface) per CLAUDE.md anti-pattern guardrails and
24	// D-C1 provider firewall.
25	//
26	// Prices are in US cents per one million tokens (cents/MTok). Sources:
27	//   claude-api skill model table, verified 2026-06-11; drift-checked weekly
28	//   by hack/check-pricing-drift.sh per D-03.
29	//
30	//   - claude-fable-5:   $10/M input, $50/M output
31	//   - claude-opus-4-8:  $5/M input,  $25/M output
32	//   - claude-opus-4-7:  $5/M input,  $25/M output
33	//   - claude-opus-4-6:  $5/M input,  $25/M output
34	//   - claude-sonnet-4-6: $3/M input, $15/M output
35	//   - claude-haiku-4-5:  $1/M input,  $5/M output
36	//
37	// Cache pricing follows the Anthropic "Prompt Caching" rate schedule:
38	//   - cacheWrite: 1.25× the base input price per model
39	//   - cacheRead:  0.10× the base input price per model
40	
41	import (
42		"fmt"
43		"os"
44	
45		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
46	)
47	
48	// modelPrice holds per-model billing rates in US cents per one million tokens.
49	type modelPrice struct {
50		inputCentsPerMTok      int64
51		outputCentsPerMTok     int64
52		cacheReadCentsPerMTok  int64
53		cacheWriteCentsPerMTok int64
54	}
55	
56	// priceTable is keyed on the exact resolved model string the orchestrator
57	// places in EnvelopeIn.Provider.Model.  Key strings must match the values in
58	// examples/projects/medium/project.yaml (subagent.model) and
59	// charts/tide/values.yaml (subagent per-level model defaults).
60	var priceTable = map[string]modelPrice{
61		// Most expensive tier — serves as conservativeTier fallback (D-01).
62		// $10/M input, $50/M output.
63		"claude-fable-5": {
64			inputCentsPerMTok:      1000,
65			outputCentsPerMTok:     5000,
66			cacheReadCentsPerMTok:  100,
67			cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 1250,
68		},
69	
70		// Opus 4.8 — $5/M input, $25/M output (D-01, verified 2026-06-11).
71		"claude-opus-4-8": {
72			inputCentsPerMTok:      500,
73			outputCentsPerMTok:     2500,
74			cacheReadCentsPerMTok:  50,
75			cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 625,
76		},
77	
78		// Opus 4.7 — $5/M input, $25/M output (D-01 correction; was $15/$75, Opus 4.1-era error).
79		"claude-opus-4-7": {
80			inputCentsPerMTok:      500,
81			outputCentsPerMTok:     2500,
82			cacheReadCentsPerMTok:  50,
83			cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 625,
84		},
85	
86		// Opus 4.6 — $5/M input, $25/M output (D-01, verified 2026-06-11).
87		"claude-opus-4-6": {
88			inputCentsPerMTok:      500,
89			outputCentsPerMTok:     2500,
90			cacheReadCentsPerMTok:  50,
91			cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 625,
92		},
93	
94		// Chart per-level defaults for phase, plan, and top-level fallback.
95		// $3/M input, $15/M output; cache rates follow the same 1.25×/0.10× rule.
96		"claude-sonnet-4-6": {
97			inputCentsPerMTok:      300,
98			outputCentsPerMTok:     1500,
99			cacheReadCentsPerMTok:  30,
100			cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 375,
101		},
102	
103		// DoD model (medium sample per-Project default, chart task-level default).
104		// $1/M input, $5/M output; cache rates: write=1.25×input, read=0.10×input.
105		"claude-haiku-4-5": {
106			inputCentsPerMTok:      100,
107			outputCentsPerMTok:     500,
108			cacheReadCentsPerMTok:  10,
109			cacheWriteCentsPerMTok: 125,
110		},
111	}
112	
113	// conservativeTier is the fallback price applied on a table miss (T-09-01 /
114	// Pitfall 4): use the most expensive known tier so a missing entry never
115	// under-counts budget spend. After D-01, fable-5 at $50/MTok output is the
116	// most expensive entry — NOT opus-4-7 (now corrected to $25/MTok).
117	var conservativeTier = priceTable["claude-fable-5"]
118	
119	// estimatedCostCents returns the estimated cost in US cents (rounded up to the
120	// nearest whole cent) for the given model and token usage.
121	//
122	// It reads a.prices — the per-instance effective table built by New() as
123	// maps.Clone(priceTable) merged with Options.PricingOverrides. The package-level
124	// priceTable is never read here (T-14-02 / Pitfall 2).
125	//
126	// On a table miss the method:
127	//  1. Logs a loud warning to stderr so the operator sees it in Pod logs.
128	//  2. Falls back to the most-expensive known tier (conservativeTier) to
129	//     ensure budget tracking never silently under-reports spend (T-09-01).
130	//
131	// A zero-token usage for a known model returns 0 (no spend).
132	func (a *Anthropic) estimatedCostCents(model string, u pkgdispatch.Usage) int64 {
133		price, ok := a.prices[model]
134		if !ok {
135			// Loud, operator-visible warning: unknown model → conservative default.
136			// Never return 0 silently (Pitfall 4 / T-09-01).
137			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "pricing: unknown model %q, using conservative default (most-expensive known tier)\n", model)
138			price = conservativeTier
139		}
140	
141		// Sum cost across all four token dimensions.
142		// Division by 1_000_000 converts from "cents per million" to actual cents.
143		// Integer arithmetic: compute the numerator first to preserve precision,
144		// then apply ceiling division (ceil(n/d) = (n + d - 1) / d).
145		numerator := u.InputTokens*price.inputCentsPerMTok +
146			u.OutputTokens*price.outputCentsPerMTok +
147			u.CacheReadTokens*price.cacheReadCentsPerMTok +
148			u.CacheCreationTokens*price.cacheWriteCentsPerMTok
149	
150		if numerator == 0 {
151			return 0
152		}
153	
154		const million = int64(1_000_000)
155		// Ceiling division: round up any sub-cent fraction to 1 cent.
156		return (numerator + million - 1) / million
157	}

17	// Package anthropic implements pkg/dispatch.Subagent against Anthropic's
18	// Claude Code CLI. Per D-C1 (the provider firewall) all Anthropic-specific
19	// code lives here, NOT in pkg/dispatch, pkg/controller, or pkg/dag â the
20	// firewall is enforced at build time by tools/analyzers/providerfirewall.
21	//
22	// HARN-06 decision (03-RESEARCH Â§"Alternatives Considered"): we shell out to
23	// the `claude` CLI rather than embedding the Anthropic Go SDK. The CLI bundles
24	// the agent loop, hooks, MCP, skills, and bash/file tools that would otherwise
25	// have to be re-implemented in Go. Phase 3 does not depend on the Anthropic
26	// Go SDK module.
27	//
28	// CLAUDE.md anti-pattern guardrails baked in:
29	//   - NEVER mount the host claude config dir â the --bare flag (RESEARCH
30	//     Â§"Critical new finding") skips auto-discovery of hooks/skills/plugins/MCP
31	//     and any CLAUDE-doc auto-memory, so the per-Pod runtime is hermetic.
32	//   - NEVER use OAuth headless â claude-code#29983, #7100 break it. We pin
33	//     ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to the signed token minted by the controller and
34	//     validated by the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4).
35	//   - NEVER embed an LLM API key directly â the API key lives only in the
36	//     credproxy sidecar; this binary sees a short-lived HMAC token.
37	package anthropic
38	
39	import (
40		"bytes"
41		"context"
42		"encoding/json"
43		"errors"
44		"fmt"
45		"maps"
46		"os"
47		"os/exec"
48		"path/filepath"
49		"sort"
50		"strings"
51		"time"
52	
53		"github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/internal/subagent/common"
54		pkgdispatch "github.com/jsquirrelz/tide/pkg/dispatch"
55	)
56	
57	// vendorSentinel is the Provider.Vendor value this binary accepts. The
58	// envelope.Provider.Vendor field is the compile-time agreement between the
59	// orchestrator-resolved provider triple and the running subagent image â if
60	// they disagree, we refuse to dispatch (Pitfall 14 mitigation).
61	const vendorSentinel = "anthropic"
62	
63	// paramsAllowList enforces Q3 RESOLVED (03-RESEARCH Â§"Open Questions" line
64	// 933): unknown Provider.Params keys are rejected at startup. The fail-fast
65	// posture catches typos at apply time rather than letting them silently
66	// disappear into a passthrough. Add new keys here only after wiring the
67	// provider-side mapping that actually consumes them.
68	var paramsAllowList = map[string]bool{
69		"temperature":     true,
70		"thinking_budget": true,
71		"top_p":           true,
72		"top_k":           true,
73	}
74	
75	// nodeExtraCACertsPath is where the credproxy sidecar mounts its self-signed
76	// CA. NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS makes the claude CLI (a Node binary) trust the
77	// 127.0.0.1:8443 reverse proxy without disabling TLS verification globally
78	// (Phase 2 D-C2).
79	const nodeExtraCACertsPath = "/etc/tide/proxy/ca.crt"
80	
81	// Options configures the Anthropic subagent. Zero values pick sensible
82	// defaults for production use; tests override individual fields.
83	type Options struct {
84		// ClaudeBinary is the path to the claude CLI. Defaults to "claude" so
85		// the image's PATH resolves it.
86		ClaudeBinary string
87	
88		// WorkspaceRoot is the per-Project PVC mount point. The per-Task
89		// events.jsonl audit log lives at
90		// <WorkspaceRoot>/envelopes/<TaskUID>/events.jsonl per Phase 3 D-B4.
91		// Defaults to "/workspace".
92		WorkspaceRoot string
93	
94		// PricingOverrides is the D-02 per-instance price override map. On New(),
95		// these entries are merged over a clone of the compiled priceTable —
96		// the package-level priceTable is never mutated (T-14-02). Zero fields
97		// in an override entry derive from the existing compiled entry (if present)
98		// or from the override's non-zero fields. Cache fields are auto-derived
99		// from input when left as 0 (read=input/10, write=input*125/100).
100		PricingOverrides map[string]pkgdispatch.PriceOverride
101	}
102	
103	// Anthropic implements pkg/dispatch.Subagent against the Claude Code CLI.
104	// Construct via [New]; instances are safe for concurrent calls because
105	// Run() makes no mutable struct state changes.
106	type Anthropic struct {
107		opts Options
108	
109		// prices is the per-instance effective price table, built by New() as
110		// maps.Clone(priceTable) merged with opts.PricingOverrides. It is read-only
111		// after construction, so concurrent Run() calls are safe. The package-level
112		// priceTable is never mutated (T-14-02 / Pitfall 2).
113		prices map[string]modelPrice
114	
115		// execFunc is the indirection seam that lets tests replace
116		// exec.CommandContext with a fixture-serving fake. Production calls
117		// New() which defaults this to exec.CommandContext; tests set it
118		// directly on the returned *Anthropic.
119		execFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd
120	}
121	
122	// New returns an *Anthropic configured with opts. Defaults: ClaudeBinary
123	// resolves "claude" via PATH; WorkspaceRoot is "/workspace".
124	//
125	// D-02: opts.PricingOverrides are merged over a clone of the compiled priceTable
126	// to build a per-instance effective table. The package-level priceTable is never
127	// mutated (T-14-02 / Pitfall 2).
128	func New(opts Options) *Anthropic {
129		if opts.ClaudeBinary == "" {
130			opts.ClaudeBinary = "claude"
131		}
132		if opts.WorkspaceRoot == "" {
133			opts.WorkspaceRoot = "/workspace"
134		}
135	
136		// Build the per-instance effective price table (D-02 / T-14-02).
137		// Clone first so we never write to the package-level var.
138		effective := maps.Clone(priceTable)
139		for modelID, override := range opts.PricingOverrides {
140			// Start from the existing compiled entry if available; otherwise zero.
141			base := effective[modelID]
142			if override.InputCentsPerMTok > 0 {
143				base.inputCentsPerMTok = override.InputCentsPerMTok
144			}
145			if override.OutputCentsPerMTok > 0 {
146				base.outputCentsPerMTok = override.OutputCentsPerMTok
147			}
148			// Cache fields: use explicit override value when > 0; otherwise derive
149			// from the (possibly just-updated) input rate.
150			if override.CacheReadCentsPerMTok > 0 {
151				base.cacheReadCentsPerMTok = override.CacheReadCentsPerMTok
152			} else if override.InputCentsPerMTok > 0 {
153				// Derive: cacheRead = 0.10 × input (same as Anthropic prompt-caching schedule).
154				base.cacheReadCentsPerMTok = override.InputCentsPerMTok / 10
155			}
156			if override.CacheWriteCentsPerMTok > 0 {
157				base.cacheWriteCentsPerMTok = override.CacheWriteCentsPerMTok
158			} else if override.InputCentsPerMTok > 0 {
159				// Derive: cacheWrite = 1.25 × input.
160				base.cacheWriteCentsPerMTok = override.InputCentsPerMTok * 125 / 100
161			}
162			effective[modelID] = base
163		}
164	
165		return &Anthropic{
166			opts:     opts,
167			prices:   effective,
168			execFunc: exec.CommandContext,
169		}
170	}
171	
172	// NewWithExec is the exported test seam: same defaults as [New] but takes an
173	// execFunc override so external packages (e.g. cmd/claude-subagent_test) can
174	// inject a fake exec without touching the unexported execFunc field. If
175	// execFunc is nil, defaults to exec.CommandContext (equivalent to [New]).
176	//
177	// Production code MUST use [New]. NewWithExec exists solely so the
178	// cmd/claude-subagent shim's tests can replicate the fake-`bash -c cat`
179	// fixture pattern from internal/subagent/anthropic/subagent_test.go without
180	// the shim having to live inside the anthropic package itself.
181	//
182	// D-02: opts.PricingOverrides are applied through [New] — the effective table
183	// is built before execFunc is overridden, so the pricing behavior is identical
184	// to the production path.
185	func NewWithExec(opts Options, execFunc func(ctx context.Context, name string, args ...string) *exec.Cmd) *Anthropic {
186		a := New(opts)
187		if execFunc != nil {
188			a.execFunc = execFunc
189		}
190		return a
191	}
192	
193	// Run satisfies pkg/dispatch.Subagent.Run. The dispatch flow:
194	//
195	//  1. Fail-fast vendor check: refuse if in.Provider.Vendor != "anthropic".
196	//  2. Fail-fast params allow-list (Q3): refuse unknown Provider.Params keys.
197	//  3. Load + render the compiled-in prompt template via
198	//     common.LoadPromptTemplate(in.Role, in.Level) â never read the host
199	//     filesystem for prompt content (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
200	//  4. Build `claude -p <rendered> --model <Provider.Model> --output-format
201	//     stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages --bare` (the --bare
202	//     flag is REQUIRED per RESEARCH Â§"Critical new finding").
203	//  5. Wire credproxy env: ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL = in.ProxyEndpoint;
204	//     ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = in.SignedToken (never the raw key);
205	//     NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS = /etc/tide/proxy/ca.crt.
206	//  6. Tee stdout through ParseStream into events.jsonl at
207	//     <WorkspaceRoot>/envelopes/<TaskUID>/events.jsonl.
208	//  7. Assemble EnvelopeOut with parsed Usage + Result.
209	//
210	// Dispatch-level errors (I/O setting up events.jsonl, exec.Cmd start
211	// failure) return non-nil error. Task-level errors (claude exits non-zero)
212	// return (EnvelopeOut{ExitCode: N, Reason: ...}, nil) per pkg/dispatch
213	// godoc: "task-level failures (subagent exited non-zero) are expressed via
214	// EnvelopeOut.ExitCode and EnvelopeOut.Reason."
215	func (a *Anthropic) Run(ctx context.Context, in pkgdispatch.EnvelopeIn) (pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut, error) {
216		// 1. Vendor fail-fast (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern: provider firewall).
217		if in.Provider.Vendor != vendorSentinel {
218			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: refusing vendor=%q (expected %q)", in.Provider.Vendor, vendorSentinel)
219		}
220	
221		// 2. Params allow-list fail-fast (Q3 RESOLVED â 03-RESEARCH line 933).
222		for key := range in.Provider.Params {
223			if !paramsAllowList[key] {
224				return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: unknown param %q (allowed: temperature, thinking_budget, top_p, top_k)", key)
225			}
226		}
227	
228		// 2.5. In-pod prompt read (defect #10b fix). If PromptPath is set, read the
229		// executor prompt artifact from the local namespace PVC and populate in.Prompt
230		// so step 3's template render resolves {{.Prompt}} correctly. This is the
231		// same-namespace read: the executor pod mounts the project PVC at WorkspaceRoot
232		// via subPath {project.UID}/workspace, so PromptPath is workspace-relative
233		// directly under WorkspaceRoot (NOT <root>/<uid>/workspace — the subPath
234		// already places the pod inside the project subtree). T-09-05 traversal defense:
235		// absolute paths and "../" escapes are rejected before any OS read.
236		if in.PromptPath != "" {
237			prompt, err := readPromptArtifact(a.opts.WorkspaceRoot, in.PromptPath)
238			if err != nil {
239				return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: read prompt artifact: %w", err)
240			}
241			in.Prompt = prompt
242		}
243	
244		// 3. Render the prompt template. Template content uses the EnvelopeIn
245		// shape as its execution context, so {{.Level}}, {{.TaskUID}},
246		// {{.Provider.Model}}, etc. are addressable.
247		tmpl, err := common.LoadPromptTemplate(in.Role, in.Level)
248		if err != nil {
249			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: load prompt template (role=%q, level=%q): %w", in.Role, in.Level, err)
250		}
251		var promptBuf bytes.Buffer
252		if err := tmpl.Execute(&promptBuf, in); err != nil {
253			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: render prompt template: %w", err)
254		}
255		renderedPrompt := promptBuf.String()
256	
257		// 4a. Compute + create the per-Task events dir BEFORE the args build: the
258		// claude invocation scopes its write capability to this dir via --add-dir
259		// (defect #7), and the planner child-CRD handoff (defect #5) writes to
260		// <eventsDir>/children/ -- the same dir readChildCRDs scans below.
261		eventsDir := filepath.Join(a.opts.WorkspaceRoot, "envelopes", in.TaskUID)
262		if err := os.MkdirAll(eventsDir, 0o755); err != nil {
263			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: mkdir events dir %q: %w", eventsDir, err)
264		}
265	
266		// 4b. Build the claude CLI invocation.
267		//   - --bare is REQUIRED per RESEARCH "Critical new finding": it skips
268		//     auto-discovery of the host claude config dir, .mcp.json, hooks,
269		//     skills, plugins, and any project CLAUDE-doc auto-memory. Hermetic by
270		//     construction.
271		//   - --permission-mode acceptEdits (defect #7) is the only headless mode
272		//     that auto-approves the Write/Edit tools; without it the planner
273		//     child-CRD Write is denied ("sandbox restrictions") and no children/
274		//     dir is ever created. bypassPermissions is sandbox-only; text-scraping
275		//     of Result was rejected for #5.
276		//   - --add-dir eventsDir (defect #7) scopes the granted write capability to
277		//     the per-Task dir only (minimal privilege -- NOT the whole workspace);
278		//     readChildCRDs traversal/Kind/empty-name guards remain the second line
279		//     of defense.
280		//   - The rendered prompt is delivered via STDIN, not as the -p positional
281		//     arg (defect #8): a large planner prompt as one argv entry risks Linux
282		//     MAX_ARG_STRLEN (128 KiB). claude -p reads the prompt from stdin when
283		//     the positional is omitted (stdin cap is 10 MB). There is no
284		//     --prompt-file flag in the pinned CLI.
285		args := []string{
286			"-p",
287			"--model", in.Provider.Model,
288			"--output-format", "stream-json",
289			"--verbose",
290			"--include-partial-messages",
291			"--permission-mode", "acceptEdits",
292			"--add-dir", eventsDir,
293			"--bare",
294		}
295		cmd := a.execFunc(ctx, a.opts.ClaudeBinary, args...)
296		cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(renderedPrompt)
297	
298		// 5. Credproxy env wiring (Phase 2 D-C1..C4). ANTHROPIC_API_KEY carries
299		// the HMAC-signed token, NEVER the raw API key -- the real key lives
300		// only in the credproxy sidecar Secret mount.
301		cmd.Env = append(cmd.Environ(),
302			"ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="+in.ProxyEndpoint,
303			"ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="+in.SignedToken,
304			"NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS="+nodeExtraCACertsPath,
305		)
306	
307		// 6. Create the events.jsonl audit log. Phase 4 OpenInference parsing
308		// reads this file untouched; we tee every line through ParseStream as
309		// it arrives.
310		eventsPath := filepath.Join(eventsDir, "events.jsonl")
311		eventsFile, err := os.Create(eventsPath)
312		if err != nil {
313			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: create events.jsonl %q: %w", eventsPath, err)
314		}
315		defer func() { _ = eventsFile.Close() }() // best-effort event sink; close error is non-actionable cleanup
316	
317		// 7. Pipe stdout â ParseStream(stdout, events.jsonl). Stderr surfaces
318		// as task-level Reason on non-zero exit.
319		stdout, err := cmd.StdoutPipe()
320		if err != nil {
321			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: stdout pipe: %w", err)
322		}
323		var stderrBuf bytes.Buffer
324		cmd.Stderr = &stderrBuf
325	
326		if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
327			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: start %s: %w", a.opts.ClaudeBinary, err)
328		}
329	
330		usage, resultText, parseErr := ParseStream(stdout, eventsFile)
331		waitErr := cmd.Wait()
332	
333		// Decide exit code + reason. Order:
334		//   - parse error on the stream â dispatch-level (return err).
335		//   - wait error: task-level (ExitCode from exec.ExitError, Reason from stderr).
336		if parseErr != nil {
337			return pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{}, fmt.Errorf("anthropic subagent: parse stream: %w", parseErr)
338		}
339	
340		// Compute cost from the per-instance effective price table before assembling
341		// EnvelopeOut. EstimatedCostCents flows into budget.RollUpUsage → Project.Status.budget.
342		// This call stays in internal/subagent/anthropic/ (provider firewall, D-C1).
343		// D-02: a.estimatedCostCents reads a.prices (the per-instance merged clone),
344		// never the package-level priceTable — T-14-02 race safety.
345		usage.EstimatedCostCents = a.estimatedCostCents(in.Provider.Model, usage)
346	
347		out := pkgdispatch.EnvelopeOut{
348			APIVersion:  pkgdispatch.APIVersionV1Alpha1,
349			Kind:        pkgdispatch.KindTaskEnvelopeOut,
350			TaskUID:     in.TaskUID,
351			Result:      resultText,
352			Usage:       usage,
353			CompletedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
354		}
355	
356		if waitErr != nil {
357			// Surface task-level failure via ExitCode + Reason (pkg/dispatch
358			// godoc contract). Do NOT return as a dispatch-level error.
359			out.ExitCode = 1
360			if exitErr, ok := waitErr.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
361				out.ExitCode = exitErr.ExitCode()
362			}
363			out.Reason = fmt.Sprintf("claude exit %d: %s", out.ExitCode, truncate(stderrBuf.String(), 256))
364			return out, nil
365		}
366	
367		// Planner file-handoff (defect #5): a planner dispatch (Role=="planner")
368		// authors child-CRD JSON files into <eventsDir>/children/*.json via the
369		// claude CLI's Write tool (the template instructs the exact path). The
370		// subagent pod has zero K8s verbs (Phase 2 D-A4) — this file handoff is the
371		// only channel from the model's output into EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs, which
372		// the controller materializes server-side. No text-scraping of Result.
373		// Executors (Role=="executor") emit no children; skip the read entirely.
374		if in.Role == "planner" {
375			// relPrefix is the workspace-relative children dir
376			// ("envelopes/<TaskUID>/children") stamped onto each child's SourcePath so
377			// the controller can set Task.Spec.PromptPath without knowing this pod's
378			// absolute PVC mount (defect #10b — prompt-as-PVC-artifact).
379			relPrefix := filepath.Join("envelopes", in.TaskUID, "children")
380			children, readErr := readChildCRDs(filepath.Join(eventsDir, "children"), relPrefix)
381			if readErr != nil {
382				// A malformed/poisoned children dir is a task-level failure, not a
383				// dispatch-level crash: the agent loop completed, but its structural
384				// output is unusable. Surface via ExitCode+Reason so the controller
385				// marks the parent Failed rather than retrying a clean dispatch.
386				out.ExitCode = 1
387				out.Reason = fmt.Sprintf("read child CRDs: %s", truncate(readErr.Error(), 256))
388				return out, nil
389			}
390			out.ChildCRDs = children
391		}
392	
393		return out, nil
394	}
395	
396	// truncate clips s to at most n bytes for embedding into EnvelopeOut.Reason.
397	// Reason is human-readable; full stderr lives in the Pod's container log.
398	func truncate(s string, n int) string {
399		if len(s) <= n {
400			return s
401		}
402		return s[:n] + "...(truncated)"
403	}
404	
405	// childKindAllowlist is the runner-side T-308 mitigation mirror of the
406	// controller's childKindAllowlist (internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go).
407	// Only these Kinds may flow from a planner's child-handoff files into
408	// EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs. A file declaring any other Kind poisons the whole
409	// batch — the controller enforces the same allowlist again at materialize
410	// time, but rejecting here gives a clearer task-level Reason and keeps a
411	// non-TIDE Kind from ever reaching the cluster's typed CRD graph.
412	var childKindAllowlist = map[string]bool{
413		"Milestone": true,
414		"Phase":     true,
415		"Plan":      true,
416		"Task":      true,
417		"Wave":      true,
418	}
419	
420	// readChildCRDs reads the planner's child-CRD handoff files from childrenDir
421	// (<workspaceRoot>/envelopes/<TaskUID>/children) and returns them as typed
422	// []ChildCRDSpec, in deterministic filename order.
423	//
424	// Contract (defect #5, file-handoff option a):
425	//   - Each *.json file in childrenDir is one ChildCRDSpec {kind, name, spec}.
426	//   - A missing childrenDir is NOT an error — it yields zero children (the
427	//     controller then surfaces the empty-output condition). This keeps an
428	//     executor-shaped or no-op planner run from failing on a clean exit.
429	//   - Path safety: only regular files whose resolved path stays within
430	//     childrenDir are read. Symlinks and any entry escaping childrenDir are
431	//     rejected (traversal defense) — the model's Write tool is constrained to
432	//     the per-task dir, but the runner does not trust that.
433	//   - Kind allowlist: every spec's Kind must be in childKindAllowlist.
434	//   - Name required: a child with an empty Name is rejected (the controller
435	//     uses it as metadata.name).
436	func readChildCRDs(childrenDir, relPrefix string) ([]pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec, error) {
437		entries, err := os.ReadDir(childrenDir)
438		if err != nil {
439			if os.IsNotExist(err) {
440				return nil, nil // no handoff dir → zero children, not an error
441			}
442			return nil, fmt.Errorf("read children dir %q: %w", childrenDir, err)
443		}
444	
445		// Canonical base for the traversal check. EvalSymlinks resolves the real
446		// directory so a symlinked childrenDir is still anchored correctly.
447		baseReal, err := filepath.EvalSymlinks(childrenDir)
448		if err != nil {
449			return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolve children dir %q: %w", childrenDir, err)
450		}
451	
452		names := make([]string, 0, len(entries))
453		for _, e := range entries {
454			if e.IsDir() {
455				continue
456			}
457			if filepath.Ext(e.Name()) != ".json" {
458				continue
459			}
460			names = append(names, e.Name())
461		}
462		sort.Strings(names) // deterministic order for stable child materialization
463	
464		children := make([]pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec, 0, len(names))
465		var parseErrs []error
466		for _, name := range names {
467			full := filepath.Join(childrenDir, name)
468	
469			// Traversal defense: reject any entry that is a symlink or whose
470			// resolved path escapes baseReal. Lstat (not Stat) so we inspect the
471			// link itself, not its target. These are security boundaries — hard-abort,
472			// NOT per-file-skip (T-10-03-A mitigation).
473			info, lerr := os.Lstat(full)
474			if lerr != nil {
475				return nil, fmt.Errorf("lstat child file %q: %w", name, lerr)
476			}
477			if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
478				return nil, fmt.Errorf("child file %q is a symlink (rejected: traversal defense)", name)
479			}
480			if !info.Mode().IsRegular() {
481				return nil, fmt.Errorf("child file %q is not a regular file (rejected)", name)
482			}
483			realPath, rerr := filepath.EvalSymlinks(full)
484			if rerr != nil {
485				return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolve child file %q: %w", name, rerr)
486			}
487			if realPath != baseReal && !strings.HasPrefix(realPath, baseReal+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
488				return nil, fmt.Errorf("child file %q resolves outside children dir (rejected: traversal defense)", name)
489			}
490	
491			data, rderr := os.ReadFile(full)
492			if rderr != nil {
493				return nil, fmt.Errorf("read child file %q: %w", name, rderr)
494			}
495	
496			// Escape raw control characters that the model left unescaped inside
497			// string literals (Phase 10 medium-DoD cascade: multi-line title/desc
498			// values produced literal '\n'/'\t' bytes → "invalid character '\\n' in
499			// string literal"). Control chars OUTSIDE strings are valid JSON
500			// whitespace and are left untouched, so pretty-printed JSON still parses.
501			data = sanitizeJSONStringControls(data)
502	
503			// Use json.Decoder instead of json.Unmarshal so trailing prose after the
504			// closing } is ignored (Decoder stops at end of first JSON value). This
505			// is the observed production failure class: model appended explanatory
506			// text after }. json.Unmarshal would return "invalid character 'W'...".
507			var spec pkgdispatch.ChildCRDSpec
508			dec := json.NewDecoder(bytes.NewReader(data))
509			if jerr := dec.Decode(&spec); jerr != nil {
510				parseErrs = append(parseErrs, fmt.Errorf("parse child file %q: %w", name, jerr))
511				continue
512			}
513			// Double-object detection (T-10-03-B mitigation): attempt to decode a
514			// second value from the stream. If a second full JSON value parses
515			// successfully, the file contains two concatenated objects — reject it so
516			// the second ChildCRDSpec cannot be silently injected. Trailing prose
517			// (e.g. "With these tasks we will...") is NOT valid JSON, so the second
518			// Decode returns a syntax error and we ignore it — trailing prose is
519			// the observed production failure class and must be tolerated.
520			var extra any
521			if extraErr := dec.Decode(&extra); extraErr == nil {
522				parseErrs = append(parseErrs, fmt.Errorf("child file %q contains extra content after JSON object", name))
523				continue
524			}
525			// Kind/name validation: correctness checks, not security boundaries —
526			// per-file-skip (append error + continue) so a bad kind/name in one file
527			// does not lose its valid siblings.
528			if !childKindAllowlist[spec.Kind] {
529				parseErrs = append(parseErrs, fmt.Errorf("child file %q declares disallowed kind %q (allowed: Milestone, Phase, Plan, Task, Wave)", name, spec.Kind))
530				continue
531			}
532			if spec.Name == "" {
533				parseErrs = append(parseErrs, fmt.Errorf("child file %q has empty name", name))
534				continue
535			}
536			// Stamp the workspace-relative origin path so the controller can wire
537			// Task.Spec.PromptPath → this artifact (defect #10b). Not model-authored.
538			spec.SourcePath = filepath.Join(relPrefix, name)
539			children = append(children, spec)
540		}
541		if len(parseErrs) > 0 {
542			// Return valid siblings alongside the joined parse errors. The caller
543			// (Run) treats a non-nil readErr as a task-level failure — children that
544			// parsed successfully are still surfaced so the error message can name
545			// exactly which file(s) failed.
546			return children, errors.Join(parseErrs...)
547		}
548		return children, nil
549	}
550	
551	// sanitizeJSONStringControls escapes raw ASCII control characters (U+0000–
552	// U+001F) that appear INSIDE JSON string literals, leaving everything else —
553	// including control chars used as structural whitespace between tokens —
554	// byte-for-byte unchanged. LLM-authored child specs routinely contain literal
555	// newlines/tabs inside multi-line title/description values; the JSON spec
556	// requires those to be escaped, so a verbatim decode fails with "invalid
557	// character '\n' in string literal". This makes such files decodable without
558	// altering valid JSON (the escape of an already-valid document is a no-op).
559	//
560	// The scan tracks string-literal state with a backslash-escape toggle so a
561	// quote that closes a string is distinguished from an escaped quote inside one.
562	func sanitizeJSONStringControls(data []byte) []byte {
563		// Fast path: a document with no control bytes at all (e.g. compact
564		// single-line JSON) is already spec-valid — return it untouched.
565		hasControl := false
566		for _, b := range data {
567			if b < 0x20 {
568				hasControl = true
569				break
570			}
571		}
572		if !hasControl {
573			return data
574		}
575	
576		out := make([]byte, 0, len(data)+16)
577		inString := false
578		escaped := false
579		for _, b := range data {
580			if !inString {
581				out = append(out, b)
582				if b == '"' {
583					inString = true
584				}
585				continue
586			}
587			// Inside a string literal.
588			if escaped {
589				out = append(out, b)
590				escaped = false
591				continue
592			}
593			switch {
594			case b == '\\':
595				out = append(out, b)
596				escaped = true
597			case b == '"':
598				out = append(out, b)
599				inString = false
600			case b < 0x20:
601				// Raw control char inside a string → escape it.
602				switch b {
603				case '\n':
604					out = append(out, '\\', 'n')
605				case '\r':
606					out = append(out, '\\', 'r')
607				case '\t':
608					out = append(out, '\\', 't')
609				case '\b':
610					out = append(out, '\\', 'b')
611				case '\f':
612					out = append(out, '\\', 'f')
613				default:
614					const hex = "0123456789abcdef"
615					out = append(out, '\\', 'u', '0', '0', hex[b>>4], hex[b&0xf])
616				}
617			default:
618				out = append(out, b)
619			}
620		}
621		return out
622	}
623	
624	// promptArtifact is the minimal shape readPromptArtifact decodes from the
625	// children JSON file. It mirrors the ChildCRDSpec {kind,name,spec} envelope
626	// but only decodes spec.prompt so unrelated spec fields are ignored.
627	type promptArtifact struct {
628		Spec struct {
629			Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
630		} `json:"spec"`
631	}
632	
633	// readPromptArtifact reads the executor prompt from the workspace-relative
634	// promptPath under base (the in-pod WorkspaceRoot). It mirrors the traversal
635	// defense from FilesystemEnvelopeReader.ReadPrompt but uses base directly —
636	// the executor pod mounts the project PVC at WorkspaceRoot via subPath
637	// {project.UID}/workspace, so PromptPath is relative to WorkspaceRoot itself
638	// (NOT <WorkspaceRoot>/<uid>/workspace). T-09-05 mitigations:
639	//
640	//   - empty promptPath → error
641	//   - absolute promptPath → rejected
642	//   - filepath.Clean then ".." prefix check → traversal rejected
643	//   - resolved full path must stay under base (second-line defense)
644	//   - empty .spec.prompt → error (defect #4 class: no silent empty-prompt dispatch)
645	func readPromptArtifact(base, promptPath string) (string, error) {
646		if promptPath == "" {
647			return "", fmt.Errorf("empty promptPath")
648		}
649		if filepath.IsAbs(promptPath) {
650			return "", fmt.Errorf("promptPath %q must be workspace-relative, not absolute", promptPath)
651		}
652		clean := filepath.Clean(promptPath)
653		if clean == ".." || strings.HasPrefix(clean, ".."+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
654			return "", fmt.Errorf("promptPath %q escapes the workspace (rejected: traversal defense)", promptPath)
655		}
656		full := filepath.Join(base, clean)
657		// Second-line defense: resolved path must remain under base.
658		if full != base && !strings.HasPrefix(full, base+string(os.PathSeparator)) {
659			return "", fmt.Errorf("promptPath %q resolves outside the workspace (rejected)", promptPath)
660		}
661		data, err := os.ReadFile(full)
662		if err != nil {
663			return "", fmt.Errorf("read prompt artifact %q: %w", full, err)
664		}
665		var pa promptArtifact
666		if err := json.Unmarshal(data, &pa); err != nil {
667			return "", fmt.Errorf("parse prompt artifact %q: %w", full, err)
668		}
669		if pa.Spec.Prompt == "" {
670			return "", fmt.Errorf("prompt artifact %q has empty .spec.prompt", full)
671		}
672		return pa.Spec.Prompt, nil
673	}
