21	// APIVersionV1Alpha1 is the envelope contract version shipped in TIDE v1alpha1.
22	// Consumers MUST reject envelopes whose apiVersion field does not match this
23	// constant via [ValidateAPIVersionKind].
24	const APIVersionV1Alpha1 = "tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1"
25	
26	// KindTaskEnvelopeIn is the "kind" discriminator for input envelopes written
27	// by the orchestrator and consumed by subagent images.
28	const KindTaskEnvelopeIn = "TaskEnvelopeIn"
29	
30	// KindTaskEnvelopeOut is the "kind" discriminator for output envelopes written
31	// by the in-pod harness and consumed by the controller on Job completion.
32	const KindTaskEnvelopeOut = "TaskEnvelopeOut"
33	
34	// EnvelopeIn is the self-contained input document written by the orchestrator
35	// to /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/in.json on the per-Project PVC. The
36	// subagent pod mounts the PVC read-only; everything the agent needs to run is
37	// in this document (D-A4). Out-of-tree subagent image authors decode this type
38	// via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] + json.Unmarshal.
39	type EnvelopeIn struct {
40		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1]. Harness MUST reject any
41		// other value via [ValidateAPIVersionKind] before consuming any field.
42		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
43	
44		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeIn]. Harness MUST reject any other value.
45		Kind string `json:"kind"`
46	
47		// TaskUID is the Kubernetes UID of the owning Task CRD object. Carried
48		// through to EnvelopeOut so the controller correlates result envelopes
49		// without scanning the PVC directory tree.
50		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
51	
52		// Role is the planner/executor selector: "planner" or "executor".
53		Role string `json:"role"`
54	
55		// Level is the hierarchy level this task operates at:
56		// "milestone" | "phase" | "plan" | "task".
57		Level string `json:"level"`
58	
59		// Prompt is the full prompt body the subagent should act on.
60		// For executor dispatches this field is empty — the executor reads the
61		// prompt in-pod from PromptPath off its own namespace PVC (defect #10b).
62		// For planner dispatches (role="planner") this field carries the resolved
63		// outcome prompt directly (the outcome prompt is not a PVC artifact).
64		Prompt string `json:"prompt"`
65	
66		// PromptPath is the workspace-relative path of the executor prompt artifact
67		// on the per-Project namespace PVC (e.g.
68		// "envelopes/<plannerUID>/children/task-01.json"). Non-empty only for
69		// executor dispatches (role="executor"). The in-pod anthropic runner reads
70		// the .spec.prompt field from this artifact and renders it as {{.Prompt}}
71		// in the compiled-in prompt template. The Manager MUST NOT read this path
72		// cross-namespace; the read happens in-pod so it uses the executor's own
73		// namespace PVC (defect #10b). Traversal-defended in-pod: absolute paths
74		// and "../" escapes are rejected before the read.
75		PromptPath string `json:"promptPath,omitempty"`
76	
77		// Branch is the per-run git branch (project.Status.Git.BranchName, format
78		// "tide/run-<project>-<unix>") the executor checks out its isolated worktree
79		// onto. Non-empty only for executor dispatches (role="executor"); planner
80		// dispatches short-circuit worktree creation (harness.EnsureWorktree). The
81		// in-pod executor reads this from in.json — the single source of truth for
82		// the worktree branch (09-09: replaced the never-written branch.txt).
83		Branch string `json:"branch,omitempty"`
84	
85		// FilesTouched is the set of repo-relative paths this task is expected to
86		// read or write. Used by the harness for output-path validation (HARN-05)
87		// and by the Plan admission webhook for file-touch consistency checks
88		// (PLAN-02).
89		FilesTouched []string `json:"filesTouched"`
90	
91		// DependsOn is the list of sibling Task names this task declares as
92		// predecessors in the execution DAG. Omitted when empty (D-A4).
93		DependsOn []string `json:"dependsOn,omitempty"`
94	
95		// DeclaredOutputPaths is the exhaustive list of PVC-relative paths the
96		// harness is permitted to write. Any write outside this set is rejected by
97		// the harness (HARN-05).
98		DeclaredOutputPaths []string `json:"declaredOutputPaths"`
99	
100		// Caps carries per-task resource limits enforced by the in-pod harness
101		// (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives these from Project.Spec.budget.
102		Caps Caps `json:"caps"`
103	
104		// ProxyEndpoint is the localhost HTTPS endpoint for the signed-token
105		// credential proxy sidecar (D-C1). Subagent sets ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL to
106		// this value; the sidecar holds the real API key.
107		ProxyEndpoint string `json:"proxyEndpoint"`
108	
109		// SignedToken is the HMAC-SHA256 token minted by the controller at
110		// Job-create time. The credential proxy validates this token before
111		// forwarding API requests (D-C3).
112		SignedToken string `json:"signedToken"`
113	
114		// Provider selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
115		// subagent image should use to satisfy this dispatch (D-C3). Value type
116		// (not pointer): every dispatch declares a provider. The dispatching
117		// reconciler resolves Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,
118		// params} → Project-level defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the
119		// resolved triple here. The provider implementation in
120		// internal/subagent/{vendor}/ rejects an envelope whose Provider.Vendor
121		// does not match its compiled-in sentinel — fail-fast defense against
122		// image-tag-vs-envelope drift.
123		//
124		// Provider.Params carries MODEL parameters only (temperature, thinking_budget,
125		// …). It is NOT a general metadata bag — the anthropic runner hard-rejects
126		// any key outside its allow-list. Dispatch metadata the planner needs to
127		// build child-CRD refs lives in [EnvelopeIn.Dispatch], not here.
128		Provider ProviderSpec `json:"provider"`
129	
130		// Dispatch carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
131		// reads to build child-CRD references (e.g. parentName → projectRef /
132		// milestoneRef / phaseRef / planRef) without querying the K8s API. It is
133		// distinct from Provider.Params, which is model-parameters-only: overloading
134		// Provider.Params with dispatch metadata tripped the anthropic runner's
135		// strict param allow-list. Pointer + omitempty so executor-level and
136		// real-Claude dispatches that don't consume it don't serialize a
137		// "dispatch: null" placeholder. Real Claude-backed subagent images may
138		// ignore this struct entirely — the real planner authors from the outcome
139		// prompt, not canned parent refs.
140		Dispatch *DispatchMeta `json:"dispatch,omitempty"`
141	
142		// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata injected by integration tests.
143		// Real Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely
144		// (RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9 / D-F1). The field is omitted from JSON when nil
145		// so production envelopes are not polluted with "dev: null".
146		Dev *Dev `json:"dev,omitempty"`
147	}
148	
149	// EnvelopeOut is the result document written by the in-pod harness to
150	// /workspace/envelopes/{task-uid}/out.json. The controller reads this file
151	// after the K8s Job terminates to record Task completion status and budget
152	// tally (D-A2).
153	type EnvelopeOut struct {
154		// APIVersion is pinned to [APIVersionV1Alpha1].
155		APIVersion string `json:"apiVersion"`
156	
157		// Kind is [KindTaskEnvelopeOut].
158		Kind string `json:"kind"`
159	
160		// TaskUID mirrors EnvelopeIn.TaskUID so the controller can correlate
161		// result envelopes without scanning directory names.
162		TaskUID string `json:"taskUID"`
163	
164		// ExitCode is the subagent process exit code: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
165		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
166	
167		// Result is a human-readable one-line summary of the task outcome. Written
168		// by the harness on success or by the stub's canned response on testMode.
169		Result string `json:"result"`
170	
171		// Reason carries a structured failure code when ExitCode != 0, e.g.
172		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit", "output-path-violation", "token-expired".
173		Reason string `json:"reason"`
174	
175		// Usage is the token and cost tally for this task, rolled up by the
176		// controller into Project.Status.budget at Task completion (D-D2).
177		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
178	
179		// Artifacts is the list of PVC-relative paths the harness confirmed were
180		// written during this task's execution. Used for audit and for the
181		// declared-vs-actual output-path comparison (HARN-05).
182		Artifacts []string `json:"artifacts"`
183	
184		// CompletedAt is the wall-clock time the harness wrote this envelope.
185		CompletedAt time.Time `json:"completedAt"`
186	
187		// ChildCRDs is the slice of typed child-CRD declarations a planner
188		// subagent emits for the orchestrator to materialize server-side (D-A1).
189		// MilestoneReconciler reads Phase entries here; PhaseReconciler reads
190		// Plan entries; PlanReconciler reads Task + Wave entries; TaskReconciler
191		// (executor level) emits no ChildCRDs. Omitted from JSON when empty so
192		// executor-level EnvelopeOut documents stay small. Consumers MUST
193		// validate ChildCRDSpec.Kind against an allowlist before server-side
194		// create — threat T-308 mitigation lives at the consumer site (plan
195		// 03-08), not in this type.
196		ChildCRDs []ChildCRDSpec `json:"childCRDs,omitempty"`
197	
198		// Git carries git-side output a push Job or a harness produces, in
199		// particular the HeadSHA the per-Project run branch was advanced to at
200		// this dispatch's level boundary. Pointer + omitempty so dispatches that
201		// don't touch git (e.g. planner Jobs whose output is consumed only via
202		// child-CRD materialization) don't serialize a "git: null" placeholder.
203		Git *GitOutput `json:"git,omitempty"`
204	
205		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored. When
206		// reading the tiny termination-message status (PodStatusEnvelopeReader),
207		// this field is populated from the TerminationStub.ChildCount JSON key so
208		// planner controllers can perform race-free succession gating without also
209		// carrying the full ChildCRDs payloads (plan 09-08 Defect B). Zero for
210		// executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners. When reading a full
211		// out.json via FilesystemEnvelopeReader the field falls back to
212		// len(ChildCRDs) in the planner controllers (non-Option-C path).
213		ChildCount int `json:"childCount,omitempty"`
214	}
215	
216	// GitOutput carries git-side output fields a dispatch produces, populated by
217	// the push Job at level-boundary push success and by executor harnesses
218	// committing per-Task worktree changes (D-B4 / D-B6). HeadSHA is the SHA the
219	// per-run branch was advanced to; ProjectReconciler patches
220	// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA from this value to enable the next push's
221	// --force-with-lease check (Pitfall 13 mitigation).
222	type GitOutput struct {
223		// HeadSHA is the 40-character hex SHA the per-run branch points to after
224		// this dispatch's level-boundary push completed. Required when Git is
225		// non-nil.
226		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA"`
227	}
228	
229	// Caps carries per-task resource limits (HARN-02). The orchestrator derives
230	// these from Project.Spec.budget and injects them into every EnvelopeIn so
231	// the in-pod harness can enforce them without querying the K8s API.
232	type Caps struct {
233		// WallClockSeconds is the maximum wall-clock seconds the subagent is
234		// permitted to run before the harness sends SIGTERM.
235		WallClockSeconds int `json:"wallClockSeconds"`
236	
237		// Iterations is the maximum number of agent loop iterations before the
238		// harness sends SIGTERM.
239		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
240	
241		// InputTokens is the maximum cumulative input tokens across all LLM calls.
242		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
243	
244		// OutputTokens is the maximum cumulative output tokens across all LLM
245		// calls in this task.
246		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
247	}
248	
249	// Usage carries the token and cost tally reported by the harness in
250	// EnvelopeOut. Rolled up by the controller into Project.Status.budget at Task
251	// completion (D-D2).
252	type Usage struct {
253		// InputTokens is the total input tokens consumed across all LLM calls
254		// during this task's execution.
255		InputTokens int64 `json:"inputTokens"`
256	
257		// OutputTokens is the total output tokens produced across all LLM calls
258		// during this task's execution.
259		OutputTokens int64 `json:"outputTokens"`
260	
261		// EstimatedCostCents is the estimated US-cent cost for this task, rounded
262		// up to the nearest cent. Used for Project.Status.budget.costSpentCents
263		// rollup.
264		EstimatedCostCents int64 `json:"estimatedCostCents"`
265	
266		// Iterations is the actual number of agent loop iterations completed.
267		Iterations int `json:"iterations"`
268	
269		// CacheReadTokens is the count of input tokens served from the provider's
270		// prompt cache (D-C5). The Anthropic stream-json
271		// usage.cache_read_input_tokens field maps directly here. Surfacing this
272		// separately lets Project.Status.budget (Phase 2 D-D2) show the cached
273		// vs uncached input mix instead of hiding cache hits inside InputTokens.
274		CacheReadTokens int64 `json:"cacheReadTokens"`
275	
276		// CacheCreationTokens is the count of input tokens spent populating the
277		// provider's prompt cache during this dispatch (D-C5). The Anthropic
278		// stream-json usage.cache_creation_input_tokens field maps directly here.
279		// Cache creation is billed at a higher rate than cache reads; separating
280		// the two prevents budget rollups from understating real spend on
281		// cache-warmup turns.
282		CacheCreationTokens int64 `json:"cacheCreationTokens"`
283	}
284	
285	// DispatchMeta carries orchestrator-side dispatch metadata the planner subagent
286	// reads to build child-CRD references without querying the K8s API. It is kept
287	// separate from [ProviderSpec.Params] (model-parameters-only) so the anthropic
288	// runner's strict param allow-list is not tripped by dispatch metadata. Real
289	// Claude-backed subagent images may ignore this struct — the real planner
290	// authors child CRDs from the outcome prompt, not canned parent refs.
291	type DispatchMeta struct {
292		// ParentName is the up-stack CRD's metadata.name. The stub planner stamps
293		// it into the child CRD's parent-spec ref (projectRef / milestoneRef /
294		// phaseRef / planRef) so the controller can wire ownership without the
295		// subagent holding a K8s client (T-07-03-03: parentName is metadata, not a
296		// secret).
297		ParentName string `json:"parentName,omitempty"`
298	}
299	
300	// Dev carries test-fixture-only metadata for the stub-subagent image. Real
301	// Claude-backed subagent images MUST ignore this struct entirely (D-F1 /
302	// RESEARCH.md Pitfall 9). It is intentionally kept in a sub-struct so the
303	// compiler makes omission explicit (pointer + omitempty on EnvelopeIn.Dev) and
304	// grep for "Dev" in production code is unambiguous.
305	type Dev struct {
306		// TestMode is the stub behavior selector. Valid values:
307		//   "success"              — writes canned result.json + declared artifacts; exit 0.
308		//   "fail-exit-1"          — writes structured failure result; exit 1.
309		//   "hang"                 — sleeps past caps.wallClockSeconds (HARN-02 test).
310		//   "exceed-output-paths"  — writes outside declaredOutputPaths (HARN-05 test).
311		// An empty string is treated as "success" by the stub.
312		TestMode string `json:"testMode,omitempty"`
313	}
314	
315	// TerminationStub is the tiny cross-namespace status carrier written to the
316	// dispatch Job's 4 KB termination message (Pod.status.containerStatuses[].
317	// state.terminated.message). It carries ONLY the fields the Manager needs to
318	// record Task completion without reading the PVC: ExitCode, Reason, Usage,
319	// the HeadSHA the run-branch was advanced to (if any), and ChildCount — the
320	// count of child CRDs the planner authored. ChildCRDs payloads and the verbose
321	// Result are intentionally excluded — they stay on the namespace-local PVC in
322	// out.json, which the in-namespace reporter Job reads (plan 09-05).
323	//
324	// ChildCount enables race-free succession gating (plan 09-08 Defect B): the
325	// controller compares observed owned children against this expected count and
326	// requeues until observed >= expected before declaring the level Succeeded.
327	//
328	// By construction this type MUST stay < 4 KB when marshalled; the test
329	// TestNewTerminationStub_StaysSmall enforces this invariant.
330	type TerminationStub struct {
331		// ExitCode mirrors EnvelopeOut.ExitCode: 0 = success, non-zero = failure.
332		ExitCode int `json:"exitCode"`
333	
334		// Reason mirrors EnvelopeOut.Reason: structured failure code (e.g.
335		// "forced-failure", "cap-hit") when ExitCode != 0.
336		Reason string `json:"reason"`
337	
338		// Usage mirrors EnvelopeOut.Usage: the token and cost tally for this
339		// dispatch, rolled up by the Manager into Project.Status.budget.
340		Usage Usage `json:"usage"`
341	
342		// HeadSHA is the git HeadSHA from EnvelopeOut.Git.HeadSHA, flattened here
343		// to avoid a nested pointer. Empty when the dispatch did not produce a git
344		// push (executor-level, non-push Jobs). The Manager copies this into
345		// Project.Status.git.lastPushedSHA for the --force-with-lease fence.
346		HeadSHA string `json:"headSHA,omitempty"`
347	
348		// ChildCount is the number of child CRDs the planner authored
349		// (len(EnvelopeOut.ChildCRDs)). Used by the four planner-level controllers
350		// as the "expected" child count for race-free succession gating: a level
351		// cannot Succeed until observed owned children >= ChildCount (plan 09-08).
352		// Zero for executor-level dispatches and genuine leaf planners.
353		ChildCount int `json:"childCount"`
354	}
355	
356	// NewTerminationStub builds a TerminationStub from a full EnvelopeOut by
357	// copying the tiny-status subset: ExitCode, Reason, Usage, Git.HeadSHA, and
358	// ChildCount (= len(out.ChildCRDs)). ChildCRD payloads, Result, and Artifacts
359	// are deliberately excluded. A nil out.Git is safe and produces an empty
360	// HeadSHA. A nil or empty ChildCRDs slice produces ChildCount == 0.
361	func NewTerminationStub(out EnvelopeOut) TerminationStub {
362		headSHA := ""
363		if out.Git != nil {
364			headSHA = out.Git.HeadSHA
365		}
366		return TerminationStub{
367			ExitCode:   out.ExitCode,
368			Reason:     out.Reason,
369			Usage:      out.Usage,
370			HeadSHA:    headSHA,
371			ChildCount: len(out.ChildCRDs),
372		}
373	}
374	
375	// ValidateAPIVersionKind checks that apiVersion equals [APIVersionV1Alpha1]
376	// and that kind equals expectedKind. It is the first call an envelope consumer
377	// must make before accessing any other field (D-A3).
378	//
379	// Returns [*UnknownAPIVersionError] if apiVersion is unrecognized.
380	// Returns [*UnknownKindError] if kind is unrecognized.
381	// Returns nil on success.
382	func ValidateAPIVersionKind(apiVersion, kind, expectedKind string) error {
383		if apiVersion != APIVersionV1Alpha1 {
384			return &UnknownAPIVersionError{APIVersion: apiVersion}
385		}
386		if kind != expectedKind {
387			return &UnknownKindError{Kind: kind}
388		}
389		return nil
390	}

21	// Subagent is the out-of-tree contract for TIDE subagent image runtimes
22	// (D-A1 / REQ-SUB-01). Out-of-tree image authors implement this interface to
23	// integrate a custom LLM backend into TIDE without modifying the orchestrator.
24	//
25	// In-repo reference implementations:
26	//   - cmd/stub-subagent — canned test responses, no LLM call (Plan 04).
27	//   - internal/harness  — cap enforcement + signed-token client + secret
28	//     redaction + output-path validation (Plan 06). The harness wraps a
29	//     concrete Subagent implementation selected at startup.
30	//
31	// The concrete Anthropic-backed implementation (Phase 3) satisfies this
32	// interface behind the provider firewall enforced by `make verify-dispatch-imports`.
33	type Subagent interface {
34		// Run executes the task described by in and returns the result envelope.
35		// The context deadline should be set to caps.wallClockSeconds + grace; the
36		// harness also enforces the cap internally via SIGTERM (HARN-02).
37		//
38		// A non-nil error indicates a dispatch-level failure (network, I/O);
39		// task-level failures (subagent exited non-zero) are expressed via
40		// EnvelopeOut.ExitCode and EnvelopeOut.Reason.
41		Run(ctx context.Context, in EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
42	}

16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	// ProviderSpec selects the LLM vendor + model + per-vendor tuning knobs the
20	// subagent image should use to satisfy a single dispatch (D-C3). It is carried
21	// on every EnvelopeIn so vendor + model are orthogonal axes from the
22	// orchestrator's point of view: the dispatching reconciler resolves
23	// Project.Spec.subagent.levels.{level}.{vendor,model,params} → Project-level
24	// defaults → Helm-chart defaults and stamps the resolved triple here. The
25	// provider-side Subagent implementation (e.g. internal/subagent/anthropic)
26	// reads Provider.Model to drive its CLI invocation and rejects an envelope
27	// whose Provider.Vendor does not match the image's compiled-in sentinel —
28	// fail-fast defense against config drift between image tag and envelope
29	// content.
30	//
31	// Params is intentionally a string→string map (not a typed struct) so future
32	// per-vendor knobs (Anthropic thinking-budget, OpenAI temperature, Google
33	// safetySettings, etc.) can land without a pkg/dispatch schema bump every
34	// time. Validation of Params keys/values is the provider implementation's
35	// responsibility — pkg/dispatch only guarantees the bytes round-trip.
36	type ProviderSpec struct {
37		// Vendor is the provider sentinel string the subagent image checks at
38		// startup. Canonical values: "anthropic", "openai", "google", "xai",
39		// "opencode". Required (no omitempty) — every dispatch declares a vendor.
40		Vendor string `json:"vendor"`
41	
42		// Model is the per-vendor model identifier passed to the provider CLI or
43		// SDK (e.g. "claude-opus-4-7", "claude-sonnet-4-6", "claude-haiku-4-5",
44		// "o1-mini"). Required (no omitempty).
45		Model string `json:"model"`
46	
47		// Params is the per-vendor tuning passthrough. Keys and values are
48		// vendor-specific; pkg/dispatch performs no semantic validation. Omitted
49		// from JSON when empty so dispatches that pass no tuning don't carry a
50		// "params: {}" placeholder.
51		Params map[string]string `json:"params,omitempty"`
52	}
53

3

5	you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	
8	    http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

11	distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	limitations under the License.
15	*/
16	
17	package dispatch
18	
19	import (
20		"encoding/json"
21		"fmt"
22		"strings"
23	)
24	
25	// PriceOverride is a provider-agnostic per-model price correction in US cents
26	// per million tokens. Zero cache fields derive from input (read=0.10x, write=1.25x).
27	//
28	// This type lives in pkg/dispatch (the provider-agnostic dispatch contract package,
29	// already imported by both cmd/manager and internal/subagent/anthropic) so the
30	// manager can validate the JSON at startup (Plan 14-05) WITHOUT importing the
31	// anthropic package — the provider firewall stays intact (D-C1).
32	type PriceOverride struct {
33		InputCentsPerMTok      int64 `json:"inputCentsPerMTok"`
34		OutputCentsPerMTok     int64 `json:"outputCentsPerMTok"`
35		CacheReadCentsPerMTok  int64 `json:"cacheReadCentsPerMTok,omitempty"`
36		CacheWriteCentsPerMTok int64 `json:"cacheWriteCentsPerMTok,omitempty"`
37	}
38	
39	// ParsePricingOverrides unmarshals s (empty or "{}" → empty map, nil error) and
40	// validates: Input and Output MUST be > 0; cache fields MUST be >= 0.
41	//
42	// Validation per ASVS V5 / threat T-14-01: reject zero or negative
43	// InputCentsPerMTok/OutputCentsPerMTok and negative cache fields, with the model
44	// ID in the error string so the operator's fix is mechanical. A bad override must
45	// not fail the session (the manager validates at startup in Plan 14-05; the subagent
46	// performs defense-in-depth validation here).
47	func ParsePricingOverrides(s string) (map[string]PriceOverride, error) {
48		s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
49		if s == "" || s == "{}" {
50			return map[string]PriceOverride{}, nil
51		}
52	
53		var raw map[string]PriceOverride
54		if err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(s), &raw); err != nil {
55			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pricing overrides: invalid JSON: %w", err)
56		}
57	
58		for modelID, p := range raw {
59			if p.InputCentsPerMTok <= 0 {
60				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
61					"pricing overrides: model %q has invalid inputCentsPerMTok %d (must be > 0)",
62					modelID, p.InputCentsPerMTok)
63			}
64			if p.OutputCentsPerMTok <= 0 {
65				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
66					"pricing overrides: model %q has invalid outputCentsPerMTok %d (must be > 0)",
67					modelID, p.OutputCentsPerMTok)
68			}
69			if p.CacheReadCentsPerMTok < 0 {
70				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
71					"pricing overrides: model %q has negative cacheReadCentsPerMTok %d (must be >= 0)",
72					modelID, p.CacheReadCentsPerMTok)
73			}
74			if p.CacheWriteCentsPerMTok < 0 {
75				return nil, fmt.Errorf(
76					"pricing overrides: model %q has negative cacheWriteCentsPerMTok %d (must be >= 0)",
77					modelID, p.CacheWriteCentsPerMTok)
78			}
79		}
80	
81		return raw, nil
82	}

1	# Milestone 03 — Heterogeneous Provider Dispatch (Codex Runtime)
2	
3	**Project:** dogfood-codex-runtime
4	**Slug:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
5	**Status:** Planned — phases dispatched
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Outcome Statement
10	
11	A TIDE operator configures a single Project whose **planner pool runs Claude**
12	and whose **executor pool runs Codex (OpenAI)**, applies it, and watches a
13	multi-phase run dispatch to completion across both providers in one set of
14	Project-scoped waves. Mixed-provider waves actually execute — not interface
15	scaffolding — and the wave-boundary failure contract is byte-for-byte identical
16	regardless of which provider ran a task. Codex spend is attributed correctly on
17	the Telemetry tab and counts against the same budget cap as Claude spend.
18	
19	The only new product surface is the **Codex subagent package**
20	(`internal/subagent/codex/`) plus the **per-level vendor switch** in the
21	controller. Everything else — the global Execution DAG, wave derivation, global
22	dispatch/failure/gates/resumption, cost/budget/telemetry/cache, and the
23	dashboard — already ships in main and is consumed unchanged.
24	
25	---
26	
27	## Architecture Decisions (locked — do not reinvent in phases)
28	
29	### D1 — Implementation location mirrors `internal/subagent/anthropic/` exactly
30	
31	`internal/subagent/codex/` ships `client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go`
32	(`Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)`), a JSONL `stream_parser.go`,
33	`pricing.go`, `doc.go` (references the D-C1 layering pattern), and `Dockerfile`
34	(multi-stage, mirrors `anthropic/Dockerfile`). All OpenAI/Codex-specific code
35	stays behind the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface. **No Codex imports** in
36	`internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/` — enforced by the existing provider
37	import firewall (`make verify-import-firewall`, `verify-dispatch-imports`).
38	
39	### D2 — Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project)
40	
41	The planner pool and the executor pool each pin a runtime via the existing
42	`Spec.Subagent.Levels.{milestone,phase,plan,task}` overrides. This is the
43	correct granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves
44	real heterogeneity within one run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity of
45	per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen): per-task selection
46	(large API surface, scheduler churn) and per-project selection (no mixing within
47	a run, defeats the goal).
48	
49	Today `resolveImage` already honors a per-level image, but `ResolveProvider`
50	(`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go`) returns `Vendor:"anthropic"`
51	unconditionally — so a Codex image is *selected* at a level but then *rejected*
52	at runtime by the image's vendor sentinel. **Wiring the per-level vendor switch
53	is part of this milestone.** Vendor resolves down the same precedence chain as
54	model/image: `Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → Helm default →
55	"anthropic"`. The Codex subagent's compiled-in sentinel is `"openai"`.
56	
57	### D3 — Credential path: Secret-ref only, env-key auth
58	
59	`OPENAI_API_KEY` (or `CODEX_API_KEY`) is delivered via a same-namespace K8s
60	Secret using the established `providerSecretRef` pattern. Never mount host
61	config, never headless OAuth (broken upstream: claude-code#29983, #7100 — the
62	same restriction applies to Codex). Secret material is **referenced by name
63	only**; chart values carry the Secret name, never the key bytes.
64	
65	### D4 — Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics
66	
67	The spec's "Failure handling at wave boundaries" applies unchanged across
68	providers and is already enforced by the global indegree model:
69	
70	- Failed task → siblings in the same wave continue (declared independent).
71	- Dependents in later waves → never dispatched (indegree never reaches zero).
72	- Non-dependents in later waves → dispatched in strict profile.
73	
74	This contract **must not change** when tasks run on different providers. No
75	parallel failure path is added — the global indegree model is the single
76	source of truth.
77	
78	### D5 — Usage accounting + cost are required, not optional
79	
80	The Codex `Run()` normalizes the OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage`,
81	or budget enforcement, `tide_cost_cents_total`, and the cache-efficiency panel
82	all read $0/blank for Codex:
83	
84	- `InputTokens`  ← `usage.prompt_tokens`
85	- `OutputTokens` ← `usage.completion_tokens`
86	- `CacheReadTokens` ← `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens`
87	  (OpenAI caching is automatic, marker-free, 1,024-token floor — there is no
88	  `cache_control` to set and no separate "cache creation" concept, so
89	  **`CacheCreationTokens` stays 0**, unlike Anthropic.)
90	- `EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` ← a Codex-side pricing table
91	  mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go` (per-model cents/MTok; `cacheRead` is
92	  OpenAI's discounted cached-input rate).
93	
94	The locked metric label set stays `{project,phase,plan,wave}`; the `task` label
95	remains **forbidden** (metriccardinality analyzer). The provider-neutral
96	`SharedContext` field on `EnvelopeIn` is plain Go struct state — Codex receives
97	it for free, no special handling.
98	
99	### D6 — Codex CLI invocation (carried verbatim — do not re-research)
100	
101	`codex exec` is first-class headless (one-shot, no TTY, final message to
102	stdout). Container-clean flags: `--ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check
103	--ignore-user-config`. `--json` emits a JSONL event stream (parsed for the
104	usage block + final message). `--output-schema <file>` constrains the final
105	response to a JSON schema — used for the mandated JSON child-CRD emission.
106	Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner sets `--sandbox workspace-write` (safe
107	inside an already-isolated pod, mirrors the Claude runner posture). Auth is
108	env-key (`OPENAI_API_KEY`). Image name mirrors the Claude convention:
109	`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` (exact tag/registry are authoring
110	decisions).
111	
112	---
113	
114	## Phase Decomposition
115	
116	Six phases. All cross-phase interface contracts — the `"openai"` vendor
117	sentinel, the `Usage` field mapping, the vendor precedence chain, the image
118	name, and the wave-failure contract — are locked in this document, so the
119	planning DAG is shallow. Phase 01 (pricing/usage) and phase 04 (vendor switch)
120	have no dependencies and dispatch together; phase 02 builds on 01, phase 05 on
121	04, phase 03 on 02; phase 06 is the milestone-wide join barrier.
122	
123	```mermaid
124	flowchart LR
125	    P1[phase-01-codex-pricing-usage]
126	    P2[phase-02-codex-subagent-core]
127	    P3[phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint]
128	    P4[phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch]
129	    P5[phase-05-chart-secret-wiring]
130	    P6[phase-06-hetero-integration]
131	    P1 --> P2
132	    P2 --> P3
133	    P4 --> P5
134	    P2 --> P6
135	    P3 --> P6
136	    P4 --> P6
137	    P5 --> P6
138	```
139	
140	### phase-01-codex-pricing-usage — *(no dependencies)*
141	
142	Pure-Go foundation for Codex cost/usage. Adds `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go`
143	(per-model cents/MTok table mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go`; `cacheRead` is the
144	discounted cached-input rate; no `cacheWrite` concept) and the usage-normalization
145	helper that maps an OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage` per D5
146	(`cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, `CacheCreationTokens` fixed at 0,
147	`EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` computed). Deliverable boundary:
148	`pricing_test.go` proves the table and cost math offline, including the
149	1,024-token cache floor and the zero-cache-creation invariant. No CLI, no
150	container, no controller touch.
151	
152	### phase-02-codex-subagent-core — *(depends on: phase-01-codex-pricing-usage)*
153	
154	The core `internal/subagent/codex/` package behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`:
155	`client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go` (`Run` shells `codex exec` with the
156	D6 flag set, schema-constrained child-CRD emission via `--output-schema`),
157	`stream_parser.go` (JSONL event stream → final message + usage block),
158	`doc.go`. Fail-fast vendor check: refuse any envelope whose `Provider.Vendor !=
159	"openai"`, mirroring the anthropic sentinel guard. Populates `Usage` via the
160	phase-01 helper. Deliverable boundary: `Run` unit-tested against a fake `codex`
161	binary / fixture JSONL stream — fully offline; any real OpenAI call is guarded
162	behind `OPENAI_API_KEY` being set. Child-CRD read/traversal defenses mirror
163	anthropic's. No controller, no chart, no `cmd/` changes.
164	
165	### phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint — *(depends on: phase-02-codex-subagent-core)*
166	
167	The runtime image and its entrypoint. Adds `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` (thin
168	shim: load `EnvelopeIn` → `codex.Run` → write `EnvelopeOut`, mirroring
169	`cmd/claude-subagent/main.go`, harness-wrapped) and
170	`internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` (multi-stage build bundling the Codex CLI
171	binary, mirroring `anthropic/Dockerfile`). Deliverable boundary: the Dockerfile
172	builds clean; the entrypoint round-trips a fixture envelope through the package
173	offline; image-build/load tooling references
174	`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`. Imports the phase-02 package only.
175	
176	### phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch — *(no dependencies)*
177	
178	The controller-side vendor switch — no Codex imports. Adds a `Vendor` field to
179	`SubagentConfig`/`LevelConfig` (CEL-validated, optional, defaults to
180	`"anthropic"`), regenerates CRDs/deepcopy, and rewires `ResolveProvider`
181	(`dispatch_helpers.go`) to resolve vendor down the precedence chain
182	`Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → Helm default → "anthropic"`,
183	replacing the unconditional `Vendor:"anthropic"`. Threads the Helm vendor
184	default through `ProviderDefaults` + `cmd/manager/env.go`. Deliverable boundary:
185	unit tests prove a Codex level resolves `Vendor:"openai"` while a Claude level
186	stays `"anthropic"` in the same Project; `make manifests`/`generate` clean;
187	`verify-import-firewall` + `verify-dispatch-imports` green (zero provider
188	leakage). The interface contract — the `"openai"` sentinel string — matches
189	phase-02 by the lock in this doc, not by a code edge.
190	
191	### phase-05-chart-secret-wiring — *(depends on: phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch)*
192	
193	Helm chart + operator-facing config. Adds the `images.codexSubagent` /
194	`subagent.defaults` block for the Codex image, the new provider-Secret **name**
195	value for `OPENAI_API_KEY`/`CODEX_API_KEY` (referenced by name only — never the
196	key bytes), the env-key injection into dispatch Job pods following the
197	`providerSecretRef` pattern, and the manager-Deployment env that feeds the
198	phase-04 Helm vendor default. Deliverable boundary: `helm template` renders the
199	codex image default, the Secret ref, and the vendor-default env across
200	enabled/disabled permutations; no secret material inlined. Consumes the phase-04
201	manager-env contract.
202	
203	### phase-06-hetero-integration — *(depends on: phase-02, phase-03, phase-04, phase-05)*
204	
205	The milestone's join barrier — authors no new feature scope. Adds the
206	heterogeneous example Project (`examples/projects/.../project.yaml`: planner
207	pool Claude, executor pool Codex) and the mixed-provider integration test that
208	dispatches a multi-phase run to completion across both providers in one set of
209	Project-scoped waves. Verifies, end-to-end: (1) the wave-boundary failure
210	contract (D4) is identical when the failed task ran on Codex — siblings
211	continue, dependents never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile;
212	(2) Codex spend is attributed on `tide_cost_cents_total` + the cache-efficiency
213	panel under the locked label set, and counts against the budget cap; (3)
214	`make test` exits 0 and every `make verify-*` guard is green. Fixes whatever the
215	join surfaces.
216	
217	---
218	
219	## Exit Criteria
220	
221	1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit and integration tests pass unmodified.
222	2. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` are green:
223	   no `internal/subagent/codex` (or OpenAI SDK) import appears in
224	   `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
225	3. In one Project, `ResolveProvider` returns `Vendor:"openai"` for a
226	   Codex-pinned level and `Vendor:"anthropic"` for a Claude-pinned level
227	   (unit test).
228	4. The Codex `Run()` rejects an envelope whose `Provider.Vendor != "openai"`
229	   (fail-fast vendor guard, unit test).
230	5. The Codex usage normalizer maps `cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, holds
231	   `CacheCreationTokens == 0`, and the pricing table computes
232	   `EstimatedCostCents`/`CacheSavingsCents` offline (unit test).
233	6. `internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` builds clean and bundles the Codex CLI.
234	7. The mixed-provider integration run reaches completion; a forced Codex task
235	   failure reproduces the D4 wave-boundary semantics byte-for-byte.
236	8. `tide_cost_cents_total` and the cache-efficiency panel attribute non-zero
237	   Codex spend under the `{project,phase,plan,wave}` label set; the `task` label
238	   is absent (metriccardinality analyzer green).
239	9. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set — the suite
240	   passes fully offline.
241	10. No secret material is inlined in any manifest or chart value — credentials
242	    are referenced by Secret name only.
243	
244	---
245	
246	## Constraints (inherited)
247	
248	- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in `internal/subagent/codex/` behind the
249	  `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface — no provider leakage into the controller
250	  or `cmd/manager`.
251	- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves; no
252	  parallel failure path — the global indegree model is authoritative.
253	- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — Secret name only.
254	- Apache 2.0 headers on all new files, logr/zap logging, CEL validation for new
255	  CRD fields, controller-runtime patterns throughout; keep all `make verify-*`
256	  guards green.
257	- Do not break existing tests; confirm `make test` is green before any plan is
258	  delivered.
259	- No real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit `OPENAI_API_KEY` env
260	  guard — tests pass offline.
261	
262	---
263	
264	## Upstream / Downstream Milestones
265	
266	- **Upstream:** v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1 — the v1alpha2 schema +
267	  global Execution DAG (Phases 23–26), global dispatch/failure/gates/resumption
268	  (Phase 25), cost/budget/telemetry/cache (Phases 16, 18–21), and the dashboard
269	  global execution-DAG view (Phase 26). All consumed as given infrastructure.
270	- **Downstream:** None currently scoped. The vendor switch generalizes to
271	  additional providers (Google, xAI) by adding a subagent package + pricing
272	  table behind the same interface — no controller change required.

usage block + final message). `--output-schema <file>` constrains the final
response to a JSON schema — used for the mandated JSON child-CRD emission.
Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner sets `--sandbox workspace-write` (safe
inside an already-isolated pod, mirrors the Claude runner posture). Auth is
env-key (`OPENAI_API_KEY`). Image name mirrors the Claude convention:
`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent` (exact tag/registry are authoring
decisions).

---

## Phase Decomposition

Six phases. All cross-phase interface contracts — the `"openai"` vendor
sentinel, the `Usage` field mapping, the vendor precedence chain, the image
name, and the wave-failure contract — are locked in this document, so the
planning DAG is shallow. Phase 01 (pricing/usage) and phase 04 (vendor switch)
have no dependencies and dispatch together; phase 02 builds on 01, phase 05 on
04, phase 03 on 02; phase 06 is the milestone-wide join barrier.

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    P1[phase-01-codex-pricing-usage]
    P2[phase-02-codex-subagent-core]
    P3[phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint]
    P4[phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch]
    P5[phase-05-chart-secret-wiring]
    P6[phase-06-hetero-integration]
    P1 --> P2
    P2 --> P3
    P4 --> P5
    P2 --> P6
    P3 --> P6
    P4 --> P6
    P5 --> P6
```

### phase-01-codex-pricing-usage — *(no dependencies)*

Pure-Go foundation for Codex cost/usage. Adds `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go`
(per-model cents/MTok table mirroring `anthropic/pricing.go`; `cacheRead` is the
discounted cached-input rate; no `cacheWrite` concept) and the usage-normalization
helper that maps an OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage` per D5
(`cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, `CacheCreationTokens` fixed at 0,
`EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` computed). Deliverable boundary:
`pricing_test.go` proves the table and cost math offline, including the
1,024-token cache floor and the zero-cache-creation invariant. No CLI, no
container, no controller touch.

### phase-02-codex-subagent-core — *(depends on: phase-01-codex-pricing-usage)*

The core `internal/subagent/codex/` package behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`:
`client.go` (Client + constructor), `run.go` (`Run` shells `codex exec` with the
D6 flag set, schema-constrained child-CRD emission via `--output-schema`),
`stream_parser.go` (JSONL event stream → final message + usage block),
`doc.go`. Fail-fast vendor check: refuse any envelope whose `Provider.Vendor !=
"openai"`, mirroring the anthropic sentinel guard. Populates `Usage` via the
phase-01 helper. Deliverable boundary: `Run` unit-tested against a fake `codex`
binary / fixture JSONL stream — fully offline; any real OpenAI call is guarded
behind `OPENAI_API_KEY` being set. Child-CRD read/traversal defenses mirror
anthropic's. No controller, no chart, no `cmd/` changes.

### phase-03-codex-image-entrypoint — *(depends on: phase-02-codex-subagent-core)*

The runtime image and its entrypoint. Adds `cmd/codex-subagent/main.go` (thin
shim: load `EnvelopeIn` → `codex.Run` → write `EnvelopeOut`, mirroring
`cmd/claude-subagent/main.go`, harness-wrapped) and
`internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` (multi-stage build bundling the Codex CLI
binary, mirroring `anthropic/Dockerfile`). Deliverable boundary: the Dockerfile
builds clean; the entrypoint round-trips a fixture envelope through the package
offline; image-build/load tooling references
`ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`. Imports the phase-02 package only.

### phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch — *(no dependencies)*

The controller-side vendor switch — no Codex imports. Adds a `Vendor` field to
`SubagentConfig`/`LevelConfig` (CEL-validated, optional, defaults to
`"anthropic"`), regenerates CRDs/deepcopy, and rewires `ResolveProvider`
(`dispatch_helpers.go`) to resolve vendor down the precedence chain
`Levels.<level>.Vendor → Spec.Subagent.Vendor → Helm default → "anthropic"`,
replacing the unconditional `Vendor:"anthropic"`. Threads the Helm vendor
default through `ProviderDefaults` + `cmd/manager/env.go`. Deliverable boundary:
unit tests prove a Codex level resolves `Vendor:"openai"` while a Claude level
stays `"anthropic"` in the same Project; `make manifests`/`generate` clean;
`verify-import-firewall` + `verify-dispatch-imports` green (zero provider
leakage). The interface contract — the `"openai"` sentinel string — matches
phase-02 by the lock in this doc, not by a code edge.

### phase-05-chart-secret-wiring — *(depends on: phase-04-per-level-vendor-switch)*

Helm chart + operator-facing config. Adds the `images.codexSubagent` /
`subagent.defaults` block for the Codex image, the new provider-Secret **name**
value for `OPENAI_API_KEY`/`CODEX_API_KEY` (referenced by name only — never the
key bytes), the env-key injection into dispatch Job pods following the
`providerSecretRef` pattern, and the manager-Deployment env that feeds the
phase-04 Helm vendor default. Deliverable boundary: `helm template` renders the
codex image default, the Secret ref, and the vendor-default env across
enabled/disabled permutations; no secret material inlined. Consumes the phase-04
manager-env contract.

### phase-06-hetero-integration — *(depends on: phase-02, phase-03, phase-04, phase-05)*

The milestone's join barrier — authors no new feature scope. Adds the
heterogeneous example Project (`examples/projects/.../project.yaml`: planner
pool Claude, executor pool Codex) and the mixed-provider integration test that
dispatches a multi-phase run to completion across both providers in one set of
Project-scoped waves. Verifies, end-to-end: (1) the wave-boundary failure
contract (D4) is identical when the failed task ran on Codex — siblings
continue, dependents never dispatch, non-dependents dispatch in strict profile;
(2) Codex spend is attributed on `tide_cost_cents_total` + the cache-efficiency
panel under the locked label set, and counts against the budget cap; (3)
`make test` exits 0 and every `make verify-*` guard is green. Fixes whatever the
join surfaces.

---

## Exit Criteria

1. `make test` exits 0 — all existing unit and integration tests pass unmodified.
2. `make verify-import-firewall` and `make verify-dispatch-imports` are green:
   no `internal/subagent/codex` (or OpenAI SDK) import appears in
   `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.
3. In one Project, `ResolveProvider` returns `Vendor:"openai"` for a
   Codex-pinned level and `Vendor:"anthropic"` for a Claude-pinned level
   (unit test).
4. The Codex `Run()` rejects an envelope whose `Provider.Vendor != "openai"`
   (fail-fast vendor guard, unit test).
5. The Codex usage normalizer maps `cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, holds
   `CacheCreationTokens == 0`, and the pricing table computes
   `EstimatedCostCents`/`CacheSavingsCents` offline (unit test).
6. `internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` builds clean and bundles the Codex CLI.
7. The mixed-provider integration run reaches completion; a forced Codex task
   failure reproduces the D4 wave-boundary semantics byte-for-byte.
8. `tide_cost_cents_total` and the cache-efficiency panel attribute non-zero
   Codex spend under the `{project,phase,plan,wave}` label set; the `task` label
   is absent (metriccardinality analyzer green).
9. No OpenAI API call runs in tests unless `OPENAI_API_KEY` is set — the suite
   passes fully offline.
10. No secret material is inlined in any manifest or chart value — credentials
    are referenced by Secret name only.

---

## Constraints (inherited)

- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in `internal/subagent/codex/` behind the
  `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface — no provider leakage into the controller
  or `cmd/manager`.
- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves; no
  parallel failure path — the global indegree model is authoritative.
- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — Secret name only.
- Apache 2.0 headers on all new files, logr/zap logging, CEL validation for new
  CRD fields, controller-runtime patterns throughout; keep all `make verify-*`
  guards green.
- Do not break existing tests; confirm `make test` is green before any plan is
  delivered.
- No real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit `OPENAI_API_KEY` env
  guard — tests pass offline.

---

## Upstream / Downstream Milestones

- **Upstream:** v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1 — the v1alpha2 schema +
  global Execution DAG (Phases 23–26), global dispatch/failure/gates/resumption
  (Phase 25), cost/budget/telemetry/cache (Phases 16, 18–21), and the dashboard
  global execution-DAG view (Phase 26). All consumed as given infrastructure.
- **Downstream:** None currently scoped. The vendor switch generalizes to
  additional providers (Google, xAI) by adding a subagent package + pricing
  table behind the same interface — no controller change required.

file.content:
# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
# Build the claude-subagent runtime image.
# Invoked from REPO ROOT:
#   docker build -t ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent:test \
#                -f images/claude-subagent/Dockerfile .
#
# Stage 1: compile a static Linux Go binary.
FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:1.26-alpine@sha256:a6a091eac01ceac4b97496fe2957a49b6cdd83365337d5f46f6f73710424e805 AS builder
ARG TARGETOS
ARG TARGETARCH

WORKDIR /workspace

# Cache the dependency layer before copying source.
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod go mod download

# Copy only what the binary needs (see .dockerignore for exclusions).
COPY pkg/dispatch/ pkg/dispatch/
COPY internal/harness/ internal/harness/
COPY internal/subagent/ internal/subagent/
COPY pkg/git/ pkg/git/
COPY cmd/claude-subagent/ cmd/claude-subagent/

# Static binary: no CGO, stripped symbols for a smaller image.
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build --mount=type=cache,target=/go/pkg/mod \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=${TARGETOS:-linux} GOARCH=${TARGETARCH} go build \
      -ldflags="-s -w" \
      -o /out/claude-subagent \
      ./cmd/claude-subagent

# Stage 2: Claude Code CLI runtime image.
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 is required because the claude CLI is a Node binary. The CLI
# version is pinned (Pitfall 3 — RESEARCH.md: never npm install without an
# explicit @<version>); pin tracks the planner's choice on the STACK floor of
# v2.1.139. The signed-token credential is injected at K8s Pod-spec time by
# the credproxy sidecar (Phase 2 D-C1..C4) — never baked into the image.
# Host configuration directories are not bind-mounted (CLAUDE.md anti-pattern).
#
# git IS required at the executor (task) level: EnsureWorktree shells out to the
# git CLI's `git worktree add` (pkg/git/worktree.go — go-git/v5 does not expose
# linked worktrees). Clone still uses go-git's pure-Go HTTP transport, but the
# per-Task worktree the executor authors into needs the real git binary, so
# node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732 (Debian) gets git installed. Prior to Phase 10 no executor ever
# ran end-to-end, so this gap stayed latent.
FROM node:22-slim@sha256:7af03b14a13c8cdd38e45058fd957bf00a72bbe17feac43b1c15a689c029c732

RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* \
    # The per-Project repo (/workspace/repo.git) is cloned by the tide-push Job
    # (uid 65532) but the executor runs as uid 1000 — git 2.35+ refuses a repo
    # it does not own ("detected dubious ownership"). The pod is the trust
    # boundary (hermetic, single-purpose), so mark all repos safe at the system
    # level. This covers our `git worktree add` shell-out AND any git the claude
    # CLI itself runs inside the worktree (whose .git points back to repo.git).
    && git config --system --add safe.directory '*'

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.npm npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.142

COPY --from=builder /out/claude-subagent /usr/local/bin/claude-subagent

# Run as non-root UID 1000 — matches D-G3 subagent harness UID requirement.
USER 1000

ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/claude-subagent"]

# infer (a) ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* image registry, (b) the Phase 1 tunables
# (plannerConcurrency, executorConcurrency, maxConcurrentReconciles), or (c)
# leaderElection block — those are project-level decisions that live here,
# version-controlled separately from helmify's regenerated output.
#
# Phase 5 will extend this with dashboard.enabled, prometheus.serviceMonitor.enabled,
# and additional OSS-distribution flags (D-E2). The structure below — helmify-emitted
# controllerManager.* keys plus hand-augmented top-level Phase 1 tunables — is the
# Phase 5-compatible foundation.

# Image refs (D-A2: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/* registry).
controllerManager:
  manager:
    args:
    - --metrics-bind-address=:8443
    - --config=/etc/tide/config.yaml
    - --watch-namespace=$(WATCH_NAMESPACE)
    - --leader-elect
    - --webhook-cert-path=/tmp/k8s-webhook-server/serving-certs
    containerSecurityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - ALL
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
    env:
      # watchNamespace = "" means watch ALL namespaces (cluster-scoped install posture,
      # AUTH-02). Set this to a specific namespace for per-namespace install — the
      # cmd/manager/main.go --watch-namespace flag wires it through to the Manager's
      # cache.ByObject namespace filter.
      watchNamespace: ""
    image:
      repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-controller
      tag: ""
      pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    podAnnotations: {}
    resources:
      limits:
        cpu: 500m
        memory: 128Mi
      requests:
        cpu: 10m
        memory: 64Mi
  podSecurityContext:
    runAsNonRoot: true
    seccompProfile:
      type: RuntimeDefault
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccount:
    annotations: {}
kubernetesClusterDomain: cluster.local
metricsService:
  ports:
  - name: https
    port: 8443
    protocol: TCP
    targetPort: 8443
  type: ClusterIP
webhookService:
  ports:
  - port: 443
    protocol: TCP
--

  stubSubagent:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-stub-subagent
    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  credProxy:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-credproxy
    tag: ""

  busybox:

  claudeSubagent:

  tideReporter:

    tag: ""
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent

# dispatch time (in MilestoneReconciler / PhaseReconciler / PlanReconciler before
# calling Dispatcher.Run):
#
#   Levels.<level>.Image (project.Spec.subagent.levels.<level>.image) ← per-level override

# do not specify per-Project caps in Project.Spec.Budget.
budget:
  # D-05: flat per-dispatch reservation estimate in cents pre-charged against the budget
  # cap at Job creation; bounds in-flight overshoot to estimate error instead of wave width;
  # 0 disables pre-charge (cap still enforced at dispatch via the BudgetBlocked condition).
  # Passed via --budget-reserve-per-dispatch-cents.
  reservePerDispatchCents: 100
  defaults:
    absoluteCapCents: 10000      # $100.00 default absolute cap
    rollingWindowCapCents: 5000  # $50.00 / 15-min rolling window default

# Phase 4 additions (Plan 04-14 — D-X3 / D-D2 / D-O6 / D-O3).
#
# All three blocks are ADDITIVE. The Phase 1 surface above is unchanged
# (CLAUDE.md "chart is FIXED contract — binary catches up to chart, never
# reverse" anti-pattern). Phase 4 chart additions land here without touching
# any pre-Phase-4 keys.

# Read-only dashboard backend (Phase 4 D-D2). Ships as a separate
# Deployment + Service + ServiceAccount + ClusterRole + ClusterRoleBinding
# under templates/dashboard-{deployment,service,rbac}.yaml. The dashboard
# ClusterRole is enforced read-only at chart-render time via
# `make helm-rbac-assert` (T-04-D2 mitigation).
#
# Default ON because the dashboard is one of the four Phase 4 deliverables;
# operators who want the controller-only install set
# `--set dashboard.enabled=false`.
dashboard:
  enabled: true
  image:
    repository: ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-dashboard
    tag: ""             # default to .Chart.AppVersion
    pullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  replicas: 1
  resources:
    requests:
      cpu: 50m
      memory: 64Mi
    limits:
      cpu: 200m

# PLAN.md — plan-02-codex-pricing-table

**Plan:** plan-02-codex-pricing-table  
**Phase:** phase-01-codex-pricing-usage (Milestone 03 — Heterogeneous Provider Dispatch)  
**Date:** 2026-06-18  
**Status:** Authoring tasks

---

## Objective

Lay the pure-Go cost/usage foundation for the Codex (OpenAI) subagent. Deliver
`internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go` (per-model pricing table mirroring
`anthropic/pricing.go`; OpenAI-specific field mapping with no `cacheWrite`
concept), a `doc.go` package declaration, and a comprehensive offline test
suite in `pricing_test.go`.

**Scope boundary:** No CLI invocation, no container, no controller, no CRD
changes. Only the new `internal/subagent/codex/` package directory and its
three files. All code stays behind the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface
firewall (D-C1).

---

## Context — What Already Exists (Do Not Rebuild)

The following infrastructure ships in main and is consumed as-is:

- `pkg/dispatch.Usage` — the provider-agnostic token/cost struct this plan
  populates. Fields: `InputTokens`, `OutputTokens`, `CacheReadTokens`,
  `CacheCreationTokens`, `EstimatedCostCents`, `Iterations`.
- `internal/subagent/anthropic/pricing.go` — the reference implementation
  this plan mirrors. Pattern: `modelPrice` struct, `priceTable` map,
  `conservativeTier` fallback, `estimatedCostCents` helper function. Mirror
  this pattern exactly.
- `pkg/dispatch.PriceOverride` and `ParsePricingOverrides` — provider-agnostic
  override mechanism used by the claude-subagent shim. Phase-02 will wire these
  for the codex shim; phase-01 does not need to call them.
- Provider firewall enforcement at `make verify-import-firewall` and
  `make verify-dispatch-imports` — zero Codex imports must appear in
  `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`.

---

## Task DAG

```
task-01-codex-pricing-impl  (wave 1, no deps)
         │
         ▼
task-02-codex-pricing-tests (wave 2, depends on task-01)
```

Two-task, two-wave plan. Wave 1 establishes the package and pricing logic;
wave 2 proves correctness with offline unit tests.

---

## Tasks

### task-01-codex-pricing-impl

**Objective:** Create the `internal/subagent/codex/` package with package
documentation (`doc.go`) and the pricing/usage-normalization implementation
(`pricing.go`).

**Files touched:**
- `internal/subagent/codex/doc.go` (create)
- `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go` (create)

**Acceptance:** `go build ./internal/subagent/codex/` succeeds. Both files are
present on disk with Apache 2.0 headers. `pricing.go` exports `NormalizeUsage`,
`EstimatedCostCents`, and `CacheSavingsCents` functions and declares at least
three model entries in the package-level `priceTable`.

---

### task-02-codex-pricing-tests

**Objective:** Create `internal/subagent/codex/pricing_test.go` with unit
tests that prove the pricing table math, usage normalization, and
`CacheSavingsCents` computation are correct — all offline, no API calls.

**Files touched:**
- `internal/subagent/codex/pricing_test.go` (create)

**Depends on:** task-01-codex-pricing-impl

**Acceptance:** `go test ./internal/subagent/codex/` exits 0 with at minimum:
- `TestNormalizeUsage` passes (field mapping + CacheCreationTokens == 0 invariant)
- `TestEstimatedCostCents_KnownModel` passes
- `TestEstimatedCostCents_UnknownModel` passes (conservative fallback ≥ any
  known-model estimate for same token count)
- `TestCacheSavingsCents` passes
- `TestPriceTable_ConservativeTierIsMostExpensive` passes

---

## Acceptance Criteria (Plan-Level)

All of the following must be verifiable from disk after both tasks complete:

1. `internal/subagent/codex/doc.go` exists with Apache 2.0 header and
   `package codex` declaration referencing D-C1.
2. `internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go` exists with Apache 2.0 header,
   `package codex`, and exports `NormalizeUsage`, `EstimatedCostCents`,
   `CacheSavingsCents`.
3. `internal/subagent/codex/pricing_test.go` exists with ≥ 5 test functions.
4. `go build ./internal/subagent/codex/` exits 0.
5. `go test ./internal/subagent/codex/` exits 0 (fully offline — no
   `OPENAI_API_KEY` required).
6. `grep -r "internal/subagent/codex" internal/controller/ cmd/manager/` returns
   empty (provider firewall intact — D-C1).

---

## Constraints

- Apache 2.0 license header on all new files.
- No real OpenAI API call in any file or test — 100% offline.
- No import of `internal/controller/`, `cmd/manager/`, or any OpenAI SDK in
  the codex package (provider firewall D-C1).
- `CacheCreationTokens` in `NormalizeUsage` output is always 0 — OpenAI
  caching is automatic (marker-free, 1,024-token floor) with no cache-write
  billing concept.
- The conservative fallback on priceTable miss must use the most expensive
  known tier (never under-report spend — T-09-01).
- `CacheSavingsCents` = cost avoided by serving `cacheReadTokens` at the
  discounted cache rate vs the full input rate. Negative savings (cacheRead
  rate ≥ input rate) clamp to 0.
EOF
